entries.title: Abhaysinha Gunvantrao Shelake
entries.excerpt: Dr. Abhaysinha Gunvantrao Shelake is a dedicated Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune. With over ten years of teaching experience, he specializes in Construction Engineering and Management. He has completed his B.E. in Civil Engineering and M.E. in Construction and Management from Savitribai Phule Pune University. he earned his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune, titled "Development of a comprehensive framework in reducing delays for predictable risks." Dr. Shelake is actively engaged in teaching both undergraduate and postgraduate students. His teaching portfolio includes key subjects like Project Management, Project Scheduling & Material Management, Earned Value Management, Project Risk Management, and Surveying & Geomatics at UG and PG level. His pedagogical approach combines practical knowledge with software applications, making use of advanced tools such as Autodesk AutoCAD, Civil 3D, REVIT, NAVISWORK, Microsoft Project, and Primavera. He is certified by Autodesk and holds credentials including a Six Sigma Green Belt, ISO 9001:2015 QMS Internal Auditor, and a Project Management Professional Plus (PMP+) certification from Simplilearn Academy. Throughout his academic career, he has guided over 24 postgraduate and undergraduate research projects. He has significantly contributed to curriculum development and academic coordination at MITWPU, holding roles such as Academic Coordinator and Website Coordinator. He is a core member of the Board of Studies Sub-Committee and the PMQIC Committee. He has also coordinated numerous national-level immersion programs and industrial visits to premier infrastructure projects, offering students valuable real-world exposure. His research primarily focuses on project risk management, scheduling delays, and the integration of advanced decision-making tools in construction management. His Ph.D. work has led to the development of innovative frameworks to improve delay prediction and mitigation in tunnel construction projects named as IRPAD (Integrated Risk Prioritization for Determination of Activity-wise Delay". He is also actively involved in exploring sustainable construction materials, the application of augmented and virtual reality, and the adoption of Building Information Modeling (BIM) technologies. He has published 18+ research papers in reputed international journals, including SCOPUS- and SCIE- indexed publications, and presented several papers in national and international conferences. Additionally, he has authored eight textbooks tailored for Civil Engineering programs under Savitribai Phule Pune University and Mumbai University. His authored books span topics such as Construction Management, Construction Safety, Smart Building Materials, and Airport & Bridge Engineering. A life member of the Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE), Dr. Shelake is committed to academic excellence, research innovation, and mentoring students toward industry-readiness.

entries.title: Abhijit Manikrao Bhagat
entries.excerpt: Abhijit Bhagat brings over two decades of industry experience, along with more than ten years of shaping young designers in the classroom. He understands the realities of the field, having worked on major projects with Mahindra & Mahindra, Bajaj Auto, and Tata Motors, before going on to establish his own architecture practice.

entries.title: Abhishek Prakash Chunawale
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Dr. Abhishek Prakash Chunawale is an Assistant Professor at the School of Computer Science and Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. With 9.5 years of teaching experience, he received his B.E. in IT from Sant Gadge Baba Amravati University in 2011, where he was a Gold Medalist, and his M.E. in IT from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2014. He has completed his PhD in Computer Engineering, and his research area is Affective Computing. He has research paper publications in prestigious international journals and conferences such as IEEE and Springer. His areas of interest include IoT, Information and Cyber Security, Social Media Privacy and Security, and AR/VR.

entries.title: Adinath Dnyandev Yadhav
entries.excerpt: Rigidity Analysis of structures, Optimization, Offshore Structures
entries.text: Adinath Dnyandev Yadhav Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Rigidity Analysis of structures, Optimization, Offshore Structures Prof. Adinath Yadhav, an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Pune. With 9 years of teaching experience under his belt, he completed his B.E. in Civil Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University and his MTech in Marine Structures from National Institute of Technology, Surathkal. He has completed Ph.D. in Structural Engineering at MITWPU.

entries.title: Akashdeep Sharma
entries.excerpt: Architecture, Spiritual Architecture, Concrete
entries.text: Akashdeep Sharma Assistant Professor Department of Design Architecture, Spiritual Architecture, Concrete Akashdeep Sharma is an Assistant Professor with over Eight years of academic and professional experience in the fields of architecture and interior design. He holds a Master of Architecture (M.Arch) in Construction Management and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Design and Architecture at Symbiosis International University (SIU). He is a Certified Architect registered with the Council of Architecture, New Delhi.

entries.title: Akshat Bhargava
entries.excerpt: Akshat Bhargava is currently an Assistant Professor in the School of Business at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India and a Doctoral Candidate at the Indian Institute of Management, Shillong, India. He holds a Bachelor of Engineering (B.E) in Information Technology (IT) degree from MJP Rohilkhand University, Bareilly and a Master of Human Resource Management (MHRM) degree from Indian Institute of Social Welfare & Business Management (IISWBM), University of Calcutta. His research interests cover talent management, leadership development in social enterprises, social stock exchanges, impact investment, performance of social enterprises, business analytics, organizational network analysis and public policy frameworks for nature positive economies. His dissertation work has been published in the journals of international repute. He has also presented papers in several International Conferences. In addition to his doctoral work, Akshat has worked for around 15 years in different private and public sector organizations.
entries.text: Akshat Bhargava Assistant Professor Department of Business Akshat Bhargava is currently an Assistant Professor in the School of Business at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India and a Doctoral Candidate at the Indian Institute of Management, Shillong, India. He holds a Bachelor of Engineering (B.E) in Information Technology (IT) degree from MJP Rohilkhand University, Bareilly and a Master of Human Resource Management (MHRM) degree from Indian Institute of Social Welfare & Business Management (IISWBM), University of Calcutta. His research interests cover talent management, leadership development in social enterprises, social stock exchanges, impact investment, performance of social enterprises, business analytics, organizational network analysis and public policy frameworks for nature positive economies. entries.title: Arun Balaso Mali
entries.excerpt: Mechanical Design, Compliant Mechanisms Condition Monitoring
entries.text: Arun Balaso Mali Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Mechanical Design, Compliant Mechanisms Condition Monitoring Mr. Arun Mali is currently working as Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering Department of Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Kothrud Pune. He is having more than 12 years of teaching experiance. He holds Masters of Engineering in Design Engineering Domain and Currently pursuing Ph. D. in Mechanical Engineering. Master's work was associated with Design and Numerical Experiment on Compliant Mechanisms. Previously worked on Flexure mechanisms in Precision Applications. Research work was coined to achieve a specific objective of demonstration of dual use of Voice Coil Motor (i.e as actuator as well as sensor) which eliminates need of sensor in precision scanning applications . It was planned to design frictionless, backlash free XY flexural mechanism and experimentally demonstrate the dual use of VCM. His research areas are Compliant Mechanisms, Mechanical Design, Vibrations and acoustics, condition monitoring of Machines etc. A devoted educator, he focuses on inspiring students to engage critically with Engineering and Technology while contributing meaningfully to the study of diverse perspectives. His commitment to fostering intellectual growth reflects his belief in the transformative power of education.

entries.title: Ashish Singh Tomar
entries.excerpt: Ashish believes that design education should be hands-on, empathetic, and rooted in real human needs. His goal is not just to teach tools and techniques, but to help students observe deeply, think critically, and build solutions that truly matter. What drives him is the joy of seeing young minds grow curious and confident. At MIT-WPU, he hopes to create a warm, collaborative learning space where students feel encouraged to experiment, fail, and learn. With his background in product development and UX, he would love to contribute to projects that blend technology with social impact, staying true to the institution's vision of nurturing responsible, innovative leaders.

entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: An academic and researcher dedicated to bridging the gap between theoretical computer science and practical, large-scale industrial application. My teaching philosophy centers on "learning by building," where students engage with real-world problems in data science and AI. I am deeply motivated by MIT-WPU’s vision of fostering research-led innovation and contributing by integrating advanced technologies such as LLM-powered recommendation systems and distributed computing into our institutional ecosystem to enhance academic outcomes and operational efficiency.

entries.excerpt: Personal Finance, Behavioural Finance, FinTech
entries.text: CA Bipin Vishvas Palande Assistant Professor Department of Commerce & Accounting Personal Finance, Behavioural Finance, FinTech Bipin is an Assistant Professor with the Department of Commerce and Accounting. He is a qualified Chartered Accountant and an alumnus of IIM Tiruchirappalli. He has also cleared NET (with JRF) in Commerce. He has more than eight years of post-qualification experience in industry as well as academia. He is currently pursuing PhD from Symbiosis International (Deemed University), Pune in the area of Personal Finance. His teaching interests include Financial Management, Cost and Management Accounting, Taxation, Business Mathematics etc.

entries.title: CA Dhaarna Rathore
entries.excerpt: Accounting, E Commerce, E Business, ERP, Finance, Management Accounting, IOT.
entries.text: CA Dhaarna Rathore Assistant Professor Department of Commerce & Accounting Accounting, E Commerce, E Business, ERP, Finance, Management Accounting, IOT. Dr. CA. Dhaarna Singh Rathore is an accomplished academic and finance professional with over 18 years of diverse experience in educational administration and financial Consulting. Currently serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT WPU University , she has a distinguished track record in teaching, curriculum development, and research, particularly in the areas of ERP implementation and commerce. Dr. Rathore holds a Ph.D. in Commerce, an MBA in Finance, M. Com and is a Chartered Accountant, bringing a rich blend of academic knowledge and practical expertise to her role. She has made significant contributions, authoring two books, numerous scholarly papers, securing two patents, and obtaining one copyright for innovative teaching methodologies. Her career is marked by innovative leadership, successful program coordination, and a commitment to fostering student growth and success.

entries.title: CA Manjiri Vishal Gadekar
entries.excerpt: Sustainability, CSR ,ESG ,Corporate Finance
entries.text: CA Manjiri Vishal Gadekar Assistant Professor Department of Business Sustainability, CSR ,ESG ,Corporate Finance CA Manjiri Vishal Gadekar is a highly accomplished Chartered Accountant and passionate educator, currently serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT-WPU. With a strong research acumen, she is also a research scholar focusing on Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives in India and their outcomes. Her expertise in this area makes her a sought-after speaker and consultant, and she has contributed to several publications on the topic. Driven by a desire to create positive change, Manjiri is dedicated to empowering her students and fellow professionals with the knowledge and skills they need to make a difference in the world.CA Manjiri has conducted several MDPs on “Finance for Non-Finance executives” for employees of several listed and private companies.

entries.title: CA Swapnil Mahesh Gupta
entries.excerpt: CA Swapnil Gupta focuses on clear, practical, and student-friendly teaching that helps learners understand concepts and apply them confidently. Her approach is exam-oriented yet concept- driven, employing simple explanations, real-world illustrations, and regular interaction to build strong fundamentals. With teaching experience in Law at the CA Foundation level, she breaks down complex legal provisions into easy and relatable examples, motivated to guide students through structured learning, discipline, and professional ethics while contributing to MIT-WPU via effective teaching, academic rigor, and outcome-based education.

entries.excerpt: POLYMER MATERIALS ,NANOMATERIALS ,COMPOSITES
entries.text: Deepali Sanjay Kulkarni Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering POLYMER MATERIALS ,NANOMATERIALS ,COMPOSITES Mrs. Deepali Kulkarni has a vast teaching experience of 28 years in the field of polymer materials, processing of composites, rubber technology, and design of equipment and machine elements. She has also conducted research on topics such as the effect of egg shell powder and fish bone powder content on the mechanical performance of PP, the effect of Nano Silica on phase transformation from Form-II to Form-I of Polybutene-1, and polyfurfuryl-based polymer concrete. Research Area POLYMER MATERIALS ,NANOMATERIALS ,COMPOSITES Publication 1)Effect of egg shell powder and fish bone powder content on mechanical performance of PP 2)Synthesis of Unsaturated Polyester Resin Based on Pet Waste 3)Synthesis Of Bio-Polyurethane Foam Using Liquefied Pine Wood 4)Effect of nano silica on phase transformation from form-II to form –I of Polybutene-1 5)Polyfurfuryl based Polymer Concrete Google Scholar Link https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=uL5abvEAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao Orcid Link https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0309-3519

entries.excerpt: Personality, Smartphone Addiction, Parenting, youth mental health, and interpersonal relationships.
entries.text: Dr. Aachal Taywade Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Personality, Smartphone Addiction, Parenting, youth mental health, and interpersonal relationships. Dr. Aachal is a distinguished academician and researcher, holding a PhD in Psychology with a specialization in Personality Factors, Psychopathology, Intelligence, and Smartphone Usage Behavior of Adolescents. A gold medalist in her MA Psychology program, she has in depth understanding of areas like Developmental Psychology, Educational Psychology, Personality Psychology, and Smartphone Addiction.

entries.title: Dr. Aaditi Khare
entries.excerpt: Mrs. Aaditi A Khare is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Liberal Arts having a Teaching experience of 10 years in the field of Psychology at undergraduate and postgraduate level. Her teaching philosophy focuses on clear understanding, practical learning, and active student participation. She is motivated by the desire to help students develop strong subject knowledge, critical thinking, and good values. She believes in creating a supportive and engaging classroom environment that encourages curiosity and continuous learning. In line with MIT-WPU’s vision, She aims to contribute through effective teaching, innovative methods, and academic commitment, helping students grow into skilled professionals and responsible individuals who can positively contribute to society. Her PhD is in the domain of undergraduate college students’ well-being.

entries.title: Dr. Aarti Renukadas Shastri
entries.excerpt: Natural Products
entries.text: Dr. Aarti Renukadas Shastri Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Natural Products Dr. Aarti Shastri is an Assistant Professor in the field of Pharmacognosy and Phytochemistry, specializing in the extraction, isolation and separation of chemical constituents from herbal drugs. She has a PhD in the same field and has extensive experience in pharmacological and phytochemical evaluation of herbal drugs/formulations for new therapeutic indications. Her research work is focused on natural products and their therapeutic uses.

entries.title: Dr. Aavishkar Chittaranjan Katti
entries.excerpt: Theoretical nonlinear optics, nonlinear dynamics, optical solitons, and photonic systems.
entries.text: Dr. Aavishkar Chittaranjan Katti Assistant Professor Department of Physics Photonics Research Group Theoretical nonlinear optics, nonlinear dynamics, optical solitons, and photonic systems. Dr. Aavishkar Katti obtained his Ph.D. in Physics from Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi, and his Master’s degree in Physics from the University of Delhi. His doctoral research specialized in theoretical nonlinear optics, with a focus on optical solitons and photonic systems.

entries.title: Dr. Abdul Nasir Khan
entries.excerpt: Ordered random variables, Bayesian inference, parameter estimation, and biostatistics.
entries.text: Dr. Abdul Nasir Khan Assistant Professor Department of Mathematics and Statistics Ordered random variables, Bayesian inference, parameter estimation, and biostatistics. Dr. Abdul Nasir Khan is an Assistant Professor in the School of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT World Peace University. He completed his M.Sc. in Statistics in 2015 from Aligarh Muslim University, Aligarh, and subsequently pursued his PhD at the same university, which he completed in 2021

entries.title: Dr. Abhijit Bendre
entries.excerpt: Cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear and galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium, and cosmic-ray dynamos.
entries.text: Dr. Abhijit Bendre Assistant Professor Department of Physics Cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear and galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium, and cosmic-ray dynamos. Abhijit B. Bendre earned his PhD in Astrophysics from the Leibniz Institute for Astrophysics in Potsdam. He has been a researcher at several institutions, including the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune, India, the Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL) in Switzerland and Scuola Normale Superiore (SNS) in Pisa in Italy. His research interests are cosmic magnetic fields, nonlinear dynamo theory, galactic dynamos, the interstellar medium (ISM), and cosmic ray dynamos. His past research focuses on exploring the mechanisms behind the generation of magnetic fields in various celestial objects and their connection to turbulence. A list of his publications can be found here :

entries.title: Dr. Abhijith B
entries.excerpt: Abhijith B serves as an Assistant Professor at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute, MIT-WPU. He holds a PhD in Cultural Studies from The English and Foreign Languages University, Hyderabad. His study concentrates on contemporary Malayalam cinema, particularly addressing issues of form, space, and subjectivity. By prioritising the ideology of form as a primary theoretical framework, his work analyses the historical transformations influencing Indian cinema in a broader context. His study examines the intertwining of geography and story as a crucial indicator of cinema's social component in the contemporary era, especially in the absence of concrete social markers under the neoliberal context. His study has been published in peer-reviewed journals, and he is presently examining the correlation between contemporary genre forms and the issue of political federalism in Indian cinema.

entries.excerpt: Biomedical Engineering, Mechanical Design and Finite Element Analysis
entries.text: Dr. Abhishek Madhukar Thote Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Biomedical Engineering, Mechanical Design and Finite Element Analysis Dr. Abhishek M. Thote is an accomplished researcher and assistant professor in Mechanical Engineering at Visvesvaraya National Institute of Technology (VNIT), Nagpur. He has a strong academic background, having completed his graduation in Mechanical Engineering in 2011 from St. Vincent Pallotti College of Engineering and Technology, Nagpur. He later pursued post-graduation in CAD-CAM specialization in 2014 from VNIT, Nagpur, and completed his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from the same institute in 2019. His Ph.D. research, titled ""Biomechanics of orthodontic tooth movement in invisible braces: mathematical and experimental approach,"" has gained widespread recognition in the field of orthodontics.

entries.title: Dr. Ajay Sahu
entries.excerpt: Dr. Ajay K. Sahu has completed his doctoral study (Ph.D.) from IIT Kharagpur with special focus on “Characterization of fracture networks from outcrop analogous: A flow simulation approach”. During his doctoral study, He has also carried out collaborative research work in the area of reservoir modelling and fluid flow simulation. He has published research papers in several reputed journals and also presented his works at several national and international conferences. He pursued his post-graduation (M. Tech) in Petroleum Engineering from IIT(ISM) Dhanbad and completed his graduation (B. Tech) in Petroleum Engineering from Dibrugarh University, Dibrugarh. His research area of interests includes Fractured Reservoir Simulation and Characterization, Flow in Porous Media, Optimized Filed Development Approaches, Enhanced Oil Recovery: Screening and Application, Matlab in Geoscience, Python for Oil and Gas. Presently, he is teaching basic and applied courses at undergraduate and post graduate. He also mentors students for their research projects. He is also an active professional member of oil and gas scientific societies like; SPE, SEG, ARMA, AAPG and EGU.

entries.title: Dr. Ajit Baburao Deore
entries.excerpt: Corrosion science, including anti-corrosive films on steel.
entries.text: Dr. Ajit Baburao Deore Assistant Professor Department of Physics Corrosion science, including anti-corrosive films on steel. Dr. Ajit B Deore is an Assistant Professor of Physics with expertise in Corrosion Techniques, particularly anti-corrosive films on steel. He completed his post-graduation in Physics with a specialization in Electronics. Dr. Deore's research interests focus on the development and application of various corrosion techniques to prevent or mitigate the damaging effects of corrosion on different materials. His research has a specific emphasis on developing anti-corrosive films on steel to increase the material's durability and lifespan. Dr. Deore is committed to advancing the field of corrosion techniques and has contributed significantly to this area through his research and publications.

entries.excerpt: She is a faculty member specializing in Operations and Supply Chain Management. She holds a Ph.D. from the University of Allahabad and an MBA from IIIT Allahabad, bringing a strong academic foundation to her work. With 2 years of corporate experience and 6 years of teaching in the field, she combines industry insights with academic rigor. Her areas of interest include process optimization, logistics management, and strategic supply chain design, with a focus on connecting theoretical knowledge to practical business challenges.

entries.text: Dr. Akshay Bura Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering 1. Corrosion in reinforced concrete structures 2. Durability and service-life assessment of concrete 3. Sustainable and low-carbon concrete materials 4. Ultra-High-Performance Concrete (UHPC) 5. Microstructural and electrochemical characterization of cementitious materials Dr. Akshay Ramesh Bura is an Assistant Professor in Civil Engineering with over 11 years of combined teaching and research experience at premier technical institutions in India. He earned his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology (SVNIT), Surat, where his doctoral research focused on carbonation-induced corrosion behaviour of reinforced concrete, with particular emphasis on CO₂ sequestration, durability assessment, and electrochemical characterization techniques. He completed his M.Tech. in Structural Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Guwahati, where he investigated the influence of fly ash on the mechanical performance and chloride-induced corrosion of reinforced concrete. He holds a B.Tech. degree in Civil Engineering from Walchand College of Engineering, Sangli, Maharashtra.

entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Alka Surendra Barhatte is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering at MIT World Peace University, Pune. She graduated in Electronics Engineering from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University, Aurangabad in 1978, and completed her post-graduation in Electronics-VLSI Design from Bharati Vidyapeeth Deemed University, Pune, India in 2006. In January 2020, she received her Doctorate in Electronics and Telecommunication from Savitribai Phule Pune University. With over 22 years of experience, including 1 year of industry experience, her areas of interest include Artificial Intelligence-Machine Learning, Automotive Electronics, Internet of Things, and technical skills include Data Structures, C, C++, Java, Python, MATLAB, Simulink, Multisim, and Embedded C.

entries.title: Dr. Alok Kumar Yadav
entries.excerpt: Dr. Alok Kumar Yadav is an Assistant Professor in the School of Business at MIT-WPU, Pune. He has completed Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in the area of Economics from the Indian Institute of Management Mumbai (IIM Mumbai), India. He completed his Master of Philosophy (M. Phil.) and master’s degree in the Economics Area. Fundamentally, he believes in a pedagogy where the knowledge cultivated at institutions has to be nurtured in real-world settings. Teaching provides him the opportunity to practice what he has researched, yet at the same time, it helps the students stay updated with the recent advancements in the field of Economics. Thus, it emerges as a win-win situation for all the institute's stakeholders. Importantly, Dr. Yadav would like to develop an innovative teaching approach which may promote progressive critical thinking and create inclusive learning experiences that align with the institution’s academic values. He has published various research papers in highly reputed journals.

entries.title: Dr. Amruta Sameer Naik
entries.excerpt: Stem cell and bone biology, regenerative medicine, tissue engineering, biomaterial scaffolds, and osteomicrobiology.
entries.text: Dr. Amruta Sameer Naik Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Stem cell and bone biology, regenerative medicine, tissue engineering, biomaterial scaffolds, and osteomicrobiology. Dr. Amruta Naik is working as an Assistant Professor at the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU, Pune. She is a goal-driven researcher and academician with a research experience of around 12 years in the field of Stem Cell Biology and Bone Biology. Dr. Amruta has received Ph.D. in Biotechnology from the University of Pune. Her research interests mainly include invention of new strategies for the application of Mesenchymal Stem Cells for the treatment of bone degenerative disorders to get better therapeutic effects. Dr. Amruta has received two research grants from DBT and DST, Govt. of India for perusing research in the field of stem cell biology and regenerative medicine. During her post-doctoral research work, she was also involved in collaborative research projects that studied the in vitro biocompatibility and osteointegration of scaffolds for bone and cartilage regeneration. She has published 14 research and review articles in peer-reviewed journals of international repute. Currently Dr. Amruta Naik is serving as a Guest Editor for Cancer Reports (Wiley) and JoVE.

entries.title: Dr. Anand Dhananjay Kulkarni
entries.excerpt: Flow Assurance, Biofuels, Biopolymers, Education
entries.text: Dr. Anand Dhananjay Kulkarni Program Coordinator (Chemical) & Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering Flow Assurance, Biofuels, Biopolymers, Education Anand D. Kulkarni is an expert in biodiesel production and optimization, with extensive experience in comparing different methods for controlling wax deposition in crude oil transportation, including magnetic field and pour point depressants. His current research is centered on the valorization of biomass through chemical and biological pathways. Specifically, he is exploring ways to upgrade waste glycerol - a byproduct of biodiesel production - into high-value products such as polyglycerol and polyhydroxy alkynoates (PHA).

entries.title: Dr. Anik Karan
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anik Karan, Ph.D., MRSC is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering at Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. His research spans across multiple fields, including biomaterials science, neuroscience, cancer biology, tissue engineering and biomedical imaging. Dr. Karan previously worked as a Postdoctoral Research Associate at the Department of Surgery and Transplant at the University of Nebraska Medical Center, Omaha, Nebraska, USA and the Department of Radiation Oncology and Department of Bioengineering at the University of Kansas Medical School and University of Kansas, contributing to studies on tissue engineering related to chronic wound healing and bone regeneration. He also has more than two years of experience in the biotechnology industry under L’Oreal, USA and Cl Lab LLC, Maryland, USA. In L’Oreal, he has worked on developing sustainable natural hair dye delivery systems through clay-based nano-carriers and in Cl Lab LLC., generating defined disease models for preclinical studies in the areas of type 2 diabetes treatment and Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction (HFpEF) treatments. He has done his Master of Science and Doctor of Philosophy in Biomedical Engineering from Louisiana Tech University, Louisiana, USA, on the topic of the development of novel biodegradable metal-organic conjugates and their applications in the areas of wound healing and cancer treatments. He has published extensively in leading Q1 journals, including Acta Biomaterialia, Materials Today Bio, Aging Research Reviews, Advanced Science and ACS Applied Nanomaterials. He serves as a review editor for several reputed Q1 journals from Elsevier, ACS, RSC, Taylor & Francis, Springer Nature, BMC, Frontiers and MDPI, including RSC Advances, RSC New Journal of Chemistry, BMC Cancer, ACS Applied Biomaterials Science and Engineering, and Springer Nature Advanced Composites and Hybrid Materials. He is a full member of the American Chemical Society (ACS), USA, Royal Society of Chemistry (RSC), UK, Sigma Xi, USA and the Biomedical Engineering Society (BMES), USA. His research focuses on advanced therapeutic treatments for chronic wounds and bone defects using various combinatorial therapeutic modalities involving various synergistic biomaterials with various non-invasive treatment methods, such as electrical impulses and focused ultrasound.

entries.title: Dr. Ankita Agarwal
entries.excerpt: Dr. Ankita Agarwal is an experienced academician and researcher who holds a PhD in Computational Biology from IIT Kharagpur and Gold medal in M. Tech. (Bioinformatics) from West Bengal University of Technology. With a background in Chemistry and Bioinformatics, she has transitioned into the field of Bioengineering and Chemical Engineering, and joined as faculty at MIT-WPU. She brings a unique blend of expertise in both experimental and computational techniques, and has published research articles in leading scientific journals. Dr. Agarwal teaches a professional elective course of Bioinformatics and regular courses such as Data Science for engineers, Python for Bioinformatics and Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Biology to undergraduate students. With more than six year of previous research experience in DBT and CSIR-funded projects on computational analysis and prediction of protein-RNA interactions in human RNA-binding proteins, Dr. Agarwal’s current interest of research is to understand the sequence-structure-function paradigm of protein-RNA complexes especially in human RNA-binding proteins. With demonstrated expertise in computational biology, Dr. Agarwal is committed to fostering innovative approaches in teaching and research, and mentoring students towards achieving their full potential.

Dr. Agarwal teaches a professional elective course of Bioinformatics and regular courses such as Data Science for engineers, Python for Bioinformatics and Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Biology to undergraduate students. With more than six year of previous research experience in DBT and CSIR-funded projects on computational analysis and prediction of protein-RNA interactions in human RNA-binding proteins, Dr. Agarwal’s current interest of research is to understand the sequence-structure-function paradigm of protein-RNA complexes especially in human RNA-binding proteins. With demonstrated expertise in computational biology, Dr. Agarwal is committed to fostering innovative approaches in teaching and research, and mentoring students towards achieving their full potential.

entries.title: Dr. Anushri Kulkarni
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: I am Dr. Anushri Nishant Kulkarni, an experienced academician with over 15 years of teaching in Electronics and Computer Engineering. I hold a Ph.D. in Wireless Sensor Networks and have published research in reputed SCOPUS-indexed journals and conferences. My teaching philosophy centers on outcome-based education, conceptual clarity, and research-driven learning. I am committed to fostering innovation, critical thinking, and ethical engineering practices among students. Actively involved in academic coordination, accreditation processes, and project mentoring, she integrates modern ICT tools and interdisciplinary approaches to prepare students for industry, research, and lifelong learning.

entries.excerpt: Management and Law
entries.text: Dr. Aparna Dattatraya Pathak Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Management and Law Dr. Aparna Pathak has been associated with MIT since April 2008, where she teaches various courses like Human Resource Management, Business Laws, Corporate Laws, Labour Laws, Organizational Behaviour, Organizational Development, and Marketing Management, among others. Prior to joining MIT, she practiced law for 9 years. Dr. Pathak has presented about 18 research papers in national and international journals and attended more than 20 national and international seminars. She was awarded the 'Ideal Teacher Award' in September 2017 for her dedication to teaching. Dr. Pathak is passionate about teaching and believes in bringing variations in teaching methodology to prepare her students to face the future with confidence. entries.title: Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka
entries.excerpt: Experimental nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, and nanophotonics.
entries.text: Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka Assistant Professor Department of Physics Quantum Science & Technology Group Photonics Research Group Experimental nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, and nanophotonics. Dr. Apurv Chaitanya Nellikka received his Integrated M.Sc. in Physics from Amrita University in 2012. He qualified for several entrance tests, including JAM, GATE, JEST, PRL (Ahmedabad), IPR (Gandhinagar), and IISc (Bangalore), before joining the Physical Research Laboratory, Ahmedabad, as a Ph.D. scholar in 2012. He earned his Ph.D. in 2016 from the Indian Institute of Technology, Gandhinagar, India. After graduation, he was a postdoctoral investigator at Tecnologico de Monterrey, Mexico, and gained research experience as a guest scientist at the Max Planck Institute for the Science of Light (Germany), Max Planck – uOttawa Centre for Extreme Quantum Photonics (Canada), and The Institute of Photonic Sciences (Spain). Before taking up his current position at MIT- WPU, he was a research professor at Tecnologico de Monterrey, Puebla, Mexico.

entries.title: Dr. Aquil Ahmad Khan
entries.excerpt: Dr. Aquil Ahmad Khan believes that education in media and communication should effectively bridge theory, practice, and critical inquiry to foster students' intellectual and professional growth. He strives to create an interactive, research-driven learning environment that connects academic concepts with real-world media practices. His teaching approach combines conceptual clarity with experiential learning, enabling students to engage in newsroom simulations, fieldwork, and collaborative projects through courses such as Television Journalism, Digital Journalism, and Media Production. Drawing on his industry experience, he enriches classroom discussions with practical insights and contemporary perspectives. Aligned with MIT World Peace University's philosophy of life transformation, Dr. Khan emphasizes ethical responsibility, social awareness, and value-based education, nurturing skilled, reflective, and socially responsible media professionals.

entries.title: Dr. Ashish Andhale
entries.excerpt: location of Economic Activity, Regional and Urban Economics, Indian Economy, International Trade.
entries.text: Dr. Ashish Andhale Assistant Professor Department of Commerce & Accounting location of Economic Activity, Regional and Urban Economics, Indian Economy, International Trade. Dr. Ashish Andhale is an Assistant Professor at the School of Economics and Commerce, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT-World Peace University. With a Ph.D. in Economics from Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, his research dives into the spatial dynamics of India's automobile component industry. His research, published in prestigious journals, explores themes from regional economics to international trade, reflecting his deep interest in the location of economic activity and spatial economics. Also, he is a tech-savvy analyst, skilled in QGIS and SPSS, always integrating cutting-edge tools into his work. His journey is a testament to his commitment to advancing economic thought and fostering a new generation of thinkers.

entries.title: Dr. Ashish Haribhau Pawar
entries.excerpt: Prof. Ashish Pawar is an Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He has 9 years of experience in teaching and research, as well as 1 year of industry experience. Prof. Pawar is currently pursuing his PhD in "Experimental study on Hybrid ECM for machining of High Temperature Titanium alloys" at Savitribai Phule Pune University. He has published more than 10 papers in the field of Robotics, Non-Conventional Machining, and Design and Optimization. Prof. Pawar has also been involved in organizing workshops and conferences, such as SMF2022. He is in charge of admissions-related activities for the School of Mechanical Engineering and is currently guiding the Electric Two Wheeler Team of MITWPU, which won the overall 1st Rank in a competition organized by IIT GOA and ATHER. He fosters a stimulating learning environment that encourages critical thinking, creativity, and collaboration. entries.title: Dr. Asim Samanta
entries.excerpt: Dr. Asim Samanta is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the School of Health Sciences and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. He holds an M. Pharm (Pharmaceutics) degree from Jadavpur University, Kolkata, India, and a PhD from the Department of Pharmacy at the National University of Singapore (NUS). He qualified GATE 2005 with an All India Rank of 46. Dr. Samanta has over two years of postdoctoral research experience at NUS and the University of the Pacific, California, USA, along with nine years of extensive industrial experience at Johnson & Johnson and Encube Ethicals. His expertise lies in Novel Drug Delivery System, Process Analytical Technology (PAT), NIR and Raman spectroscopy, chemometrics, multivariate data analysis, and Quality by Design (QbD). He has published widely in reputed international journals and has presented his research at numerous national and international conferences.

entries.title: Dr. Atanu Hazarika
entries.excerpt: Dr. Atanu Hazarika is an Assistant Professor at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. He is a Post-Doctoral Fellow from the Indian Institute of Management Bangalore. The title of the Post Doctoral research is ‘Developing sustainability-related knowledge and capability for farmers, Case Study of an Irrigation Tank project’. The project is sponsored by the Hanns Seidel Stiftung foundation (HSS), Germany. His PhD has been awarded by the Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur. His doctoral research work has focused on the issues of productivity and efficiency of paddy farming in Assam. His attempts to develop an interdependence (multivariate) framework for measuring the adoption rate of multiple modern technology practices. Additionally, the study attempts to find the technical efficiency and allocative efficiency level of the farmers. In addition, he worked on several projects sponsored by national and international agencies like NOVOD, SANDEE, ICSSR, and NABARD during his PhD tenure. The projects experienced give an exposure to work in different domains like the adoption and expansion behaviour of the Jatropha farmers, the economic valuation of biodiesel, the evaluation of watershed programs, and the financial risk attitude of the handloom micro-entrepreneur. He has worked as a Guest Faculty at Tezpur University, Assam. His teaching interests are Micro-economics, Macro-economics, Econometrics, Statistics, Development Economics, Operations Research, and Mathematical Economics. His research interests lie in the areas of Economics of Decision Choice, Efficiency and Productivity Analyses, Biofuel, Supply Chain, and Economic Valuation.

His primary research interests lie in the development and fabrication of composites for tribological and mechanical applications. Dr. Sahoo holds a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering, an M.Tech. in Metallurgical and Materials Engineering, and a B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering. He has published 18 papers in international journals and has one year of experience in his field. Dr. Sahoo is also a member of The Indian Society for Technical Education and teaches subjects such as Advanced Manufacturing Techniques, Materials Science, and Design Thinking Laboratory. entries.title: Dr. Bishnu Prasad Mohapatra
entries.excerpt: Dr. Bishnu Prasad Mohapatra is presently working as Assistant Professor in School of Liberal Arts, Dr. Viswanath Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune and involved in Teaching and Research in Political Science. Dr. Mohapatra has about 25 years of experience in Teaching and Research in Political Science and peace studies. Specialised in governance, public policy, decentralisation, local democracy and economic development. He has obtained M.Phil in Political Science from Berhampur University, Odisha, India and completed his PhD from Centre for Economic and Social Studies (CESS) Hyderabad in Political Science in 2018. Dr. Mohapatra has been extensively worked in Research Studies in various parts in India. He has published 28 numbers of Journal Articles in various reputed journals apart from 2 Books and 9 Chapters in Edited Books. He has also published 3 Research Monographs and associated with many global professional bodies, policy think-tanks and Journals as Mentor, Editorial Board Member, Advisor and Expert Members.

entries.excerpt: Bio-Mechanical Engineering Applications.
entries.text: Dr. Chaitanya Girish Burande Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Bio-Mechanical Engineering Applications. Prof. Chaitanya Girish Burande is a mechanical engineer with a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering (pursuing) and a Master's degree in Mechanical Engineering - Design. He has 6 years of experience, with 5 years in teaching and 1 year in the industry. His research area mainly focuses on Biomechanical Applications. Prof. Burande's expertise lies in subjects such as Design of Machine Elements, Theory of Machine, Mechanical System Design, and Dynamics of Machinery. His aim is to impart his knowledge and skills to students and contribute to the field of mechanical engineering through research.

entries.title: Dr. Chandrashekhar Digambarrao Bobade
entries.excerpt: Biomaterials, thin polymeric films, bioengineering, nanotechnology, and advanced drug delivery systems
entries.text: Dr. Chandrashekhar Digambarrao Bobade Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Biomaterials, thin polymeric films, bioengineering, nanotechnology, and advanced drug delivery systems Dr. Chandrashekhar D. Bobade is an accomplished academician, researcher, and academic leader and career blog writer with over 17 years of experience in pharmaceutical education and research. He is a DHR–ICMR International Fellow (Government of India, 2025–26) and a DST-SERB & INSA Travel Grant Awardee (Government of India, 2023, USA). Currently, he serves as the President of the Indian Pharmaceutical Association (IPA), Pune (2024–26).

entries.title: Dr. Chetan Bhimrao Khadse
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: AI, Power System, IoT, Machine Learning, Smart Grid
entries.text: Dr. Chetan Bhimrao Khadse Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering AI, Power System, IoT, Machine Learning, Smart Grid Chetan B Khadse is an accomplished Assistant Professor in the School of Electrical Engineering at MITWPU, Pune, India. He holds a PhD in "AI applications in Electrical Engineering" from Visvesvaraya National Institute of Technology Nagpur, India, an MTech in Power System from Shivaji University, India, and a Bachelor's from Amravati University, India. With a research focus on artificial intelligence, power systems, power quality, optimization algorithms, and intelligent systems, he has published over 18 research papers in peer-reviewed reputed journals, chapters, and conferences. Dr. Khadse is an active participant in MITWPU's center of excellence for electric vehicles and uses AI techniques to improve EV performance. He also regularly reviews papers for IEEE, Elsevier, Taylor & Francis, and Willey journals.

entries.title: Dr. Chinmay Devidas Deshmukh
entries.excerpt: Preclinical screening of diabetes and Inflammatory Disease models
entries.text: Dr. Chinmay Devidas Deshmukh Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Preclinical screening of diabetes and Inflammatory Disease models Dr. Chinmay D. Deshmukh is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Pharmacology in the School of Health Sciences and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Kothrud, Pune, India. He holds a B.Pharm degree from the Institute of Pharmaceutical Education and Research, Borgaon (Meghe), Wardha, Maharashtra, which he completed in 2005 with first division. Later, in 2007, he completed the Executive Master’s Program in Pharmacy Management (MBA) from Mitcon Institute of Management, Balewadi, Pune, Maharashtra. He also holds a Master of Pharmacy degree in Pharmacology, which he completed in 2009 with first division from Jawaharlal Nehru Medical College, Belgaum, Karnataka. He has been teaching for the last 14 years and completed his Ph.D. in Pharmacology in 2017 from Pacific Academy of Higher Education and Research (PAHER), Udaipur, Rajasthan.

He has a passion for exploring the properties and characteristics of semiconductors, and has published several research papers in renowned international journals. His research findings have been appreciated in the scientific community, and he continues to strive towards new discoveries and advancements in this field. entries.title: Dr. Divyanshi Pal
entries.excerpt: Dr. Divyanshi Pal is an Assistant Professor at MIT-World Peace University with a Ph.D. in Marketing from RGIPT. She specializes in consumer behavior, retail experiences, and brand management. She has published in reputed ABDC-listed journals such as International Journal of Retail & Distribution Management and Asia Pacific Journal of Marketing and Logistics. Dr. Pal has designed and taught several BBA and MBA courses and actively mentors students. She is UGC-NET qualified and has presented at conferences hosted by IIMs and IITs. Her research explores sensory marketing, consumer intention, and experiences in retail.

entries.title: Dr. Ganesh Bhaurao Choudhari
entries.excerpt: Dr. Ganesh Bhaurao Choudhari is an Assistant Professor with a M. Pharm. in Pharmaceutical Chemistry and a submitted Ph. D. With over 12.6 years of experience in undergraduate academics, 7 years in postgraduate academics, and 3 years in industry, he is a seasoned professional in his field. He has 10 research publications, 4 review/special articles, and 3 patents to his credit. Dr. Choudhari has also presented 8 papers nationally, 9 papers internationally, and 4 papers regionally. He has attended 17 STTPs/Workshops/FDPs and has received research grants of 1.60 lakhs from BCUD, Savitribai Phule Pune University and research seed fund of 3 lakhs from Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune for his research projects. He has successfully completed over 15 consultancy projects and training sessions on flash chromatography.

entries.text: Dr. Heenakshi Bhansali Assistant Professor Department of Clinical Sciences Neuropsychology, Clinical and Cognitive psychology, Socio-developmental psychology Dr. Heenakshi Bhansali is a dedicated researcher with a strong background in various domains of psychology, including neuropsychology, cognitive psychology, clinical psychology, and counselling psychology. She has extensive experience in studying the relationship between brain function, personality, and behavior, and is committed to improving the lives of individuals with mental health disorders. Some of her research has been published in psychology and multidisciplinary journals, and she has presented her findings at numerous national and international conferences. As an Assistant Professor, Dr. Bhansali continues to mentor and inspire young researchers in their dissertation research.

entries.title: Dr. Jivan Pandharinath Biradar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Jivan Biradar is an experienced assistant professor of economics in the School of Economics and Commerce at MIT-WPU. He holds a Ph.D. and M.Phil. in economics from Pune University, and has over 10 years of teaching experience. Prior to joining MIT, he worked at the Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, where he conducted research on various projects sponsored by NABARD and the Ministry of Agriculture, Government of India. His areas of research include banking, health insurance, rural development, agriculture finance, and public policy. He teaches microeconomics, macroeconomics, and managerial economics to undergraduate and postgraduate students.

entries.title: Dr. Jyotsna Srivastava
entries.excerpt: Peace and Conflict Studies, Migration Studies, Gender Studies, Masculinity, mobility and aspirations, gendered geography, and public spaces.
entries.text: Dr. Jyotsna Srivastava Assistant Professor Department of Peace Studies Peace and Conflict Studies, Migration Studies, Gender Studies, Masculinity, mobility and aspirations, gendered geography, and public spaces. Dr. Jyotsna Srivastava holds a Ph.D. in Peace Research from Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi. Her Ph.D. thesis asses existing forms of masculinities producing differential treatment for women migrants. The empirical part explores migrant women’s experiences with masculinity, division of labor, hierarchies, and the social conflict it produces. It was designed to convey the diverse experiences of women migrants in the varying masculinities present, building an alternative experiential knowledge system, stimulating the prevailing framework of gendered migration, and the role of masculinities in social-behavioral patterns during migration. She has qualified for UGC JRF-NET. She was awarded the Linnaeus Palme Scholarship issued by the Swedish International Development Agency during her research. She holds a master's degree in Conflict Management and Development. She has also attended the Summer Intensive Learning Course on: "Religion and Foreign Policy: Investigating Religious Minorities and Human Rights" at McGill University, Montreal, Canada. She has published her research work in the UGC care list and peer-reviewed national and international journals. She has attended I

entries.title: Dr. Kausik Bhattacharyya
entries.excerpt: Infectious-disease biology, microbial and viral genomics, bioinformatics, host–pathogen interactions, and antimicrobial resistance.
entries.text: Dr. Kausik Bhattacharyya Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Infectious-disease biology, microbial and viral genomics, bioinformatics, host–pathogen interactions, and antimicrobial resistance. Dr. Kausik Bhattacharyya is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Biosciences and Technology (DoBT) and Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation. Serving additionally as a Departmental Placement coordinator for the department of Biosciences and Technology, Dr Kausik has more than 12 years of research experience in Infectious Disease biology, Microbiology, Genomics and Bioinformatics and 7 years of teaching experience. He has completed his Ph.D. from Department of Biomedical Sciences (ACBR), University of Delhi. During his Ph.D., he has demonstrated how genomic sequence and novel genomic variations can have functional significance in Multi-Drug Resistant (MDR) clinical isolates of M. tuberculosis through Bioinformatics and Experimental approaches, including altering protein 3D structure, causing drug resistance, altering host-pathogen interactions, Molecular mimicry, and Evasion of Immune responses. Before joining Ph.D., he worked as a junior Team Mentor and Project Fellow in the area of drug discovery against Tuberculosis (OSDD), a CSIR-led Team India Consortium with global participation. entries.title: Dr. Ketaki Hanamant Kulkarni
entries.excerpt: Dr. Ketaki Kulkarni is an esteemed Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT WPU, Pune. She graduated from Government College of Engineering, Aurangabad in Civil Engineering in the year 2003, and then completed her M.E in Civil-Water Resources Engineering from Government Engineering College, Aurangabad in 2005. She was selected for a research fellowship in Indian Institute of Technology, Mumbai, and was a research associate there until 2007. Prof. Dr. Kulkarni has been associated with MIT since 2007 and has taught a variety of subjects like Basic Civil and Environmental Engineering, Fluid Mechanics I, II, Momentum Transfer, Hydrology and Water Resources Engineering, Dams and Hydraulic Structures. She earned her doctorate in Civil Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University, specializing in Hydraulic Engineering under the guidance of Dr. G. A. Hinge. She has published approximately 4 international journals and 8 international conference papers, including a BEST PAPER AWARD. She is a life member of the Indian Society of Hydraulics (ISH) and the Indian Society of Technical Education (ISTE). She is also serving on the editorial board of LARHYSS journal, which is a Scopus-indexed international journal. Additionally, she is a reviewer for Water Science and Technology - Water Supply, a SCIE/WOS journal rated in the Q2 journal category.

entries.title: Dr. Kumar Abhijeet Raj
entries.excerpt: Dr. Kumar Abhijeet Raj obtained his Ph.D. in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad. His doctoral research focused on the development of Viscoelastic Surfactant (VES) fracturing fluids for high- temperature reservoirs, integrating key parameters to enhance temperature stability and proppant- carrying capacity. As part of his study, he designed and conducted minifrac tests using specially formulated fracturing fluids and custom-built experimental setups to interpret critical fracturing parameters. He completed both his B.Tech and M.Tech also in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad.

entries.title: Dr. Kunal Modak
entries.excerpt: Dr. Kunal Modak holds a Ph.D. in Structural Geology from IIT ISM Dhanbad, where he conducted research on the structural geometry, structural mapping, deformation, paleopeizometry, and metamorphism of the Mesoproterozoic Kaladgi Basin. His work provided critical insights into basin evolution and its implications for hydrocarbon and mineral exploration. He also holds an MSc in Geology and an M.Tech in Petroleum Exploration from IIT ISM Dhanbad, equipping him with a strong foundation in both fundamental geological processes and their applications in petroleum systems. He has over 6 years of experience as a scientist under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, where he worked on the prestigious "Scientific Deep Drilling in Koyna Intraplate Seismic Zone" project. His research in this project focused on understanding the mechanisms of reservoir-triggered seismicity through core logging, petrography, microstructural analysis, SEM, and EDS. His research contributions include both single-author and leading-author publications, including a 2022 paper proposing a hypothetical 5 km deep borehole in the Koyna region to study reservoir-triggered seismicity. He also serves as a reviewer for several reputed peer-reviewed journals, contributing to the advancement of geoscientific research.

entries.title: Dr. Mandar Bopardikar
entries.excerpt: Molecular biophysics and biochemistry, protein aggregation, NMR spectroscopy, fluorescence methods, and neurodegenerative-disease models.
entries.text: Dr. Mandar Bopardikar Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Molecular biophysics and biochemistry, protein aggregation, NMR spectroscopy, fluorescence methods, and neurodegenerative-disease models. Neurodegenerative disorders, characterized by the accumulation of protein aggregates in the human brain, represent a significant threat to human well-being for which no therapeutic treatment is available. Dr. Mandar's research attempts to address this condition by exploring the effect of herbal extracts on protein aggregation. During his doctoral research, he discovered an Ayurvedic extract known as Triphala to be capable of inhibiting the formation of protein aggregates in vitro. Further, the very challenging task of identification of the active ingredient of Triphala was successfully achieved and the mechanistic insights into the inhibition process were obtained. After his PhD, he worked as an industrial R&D scientist on the development of generic equivalent of the following: (a) Peptide hormones involved in controlling blood glucose levels (b) Peptides to treat short bowel syndrome (c) Vitamin supplements. During his short tenure at IISER Berhampur, he was in the pursuit of unravelling the structure and dynamics of complex protein enzymes by NMR spectroscopy. entries.title: Dr. Meera JeevanReddy Chintala
entries.excerpt: Dr. Miss Meera currently works as an assistant professor of civil engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India, and heads Building Materials & Sustainability (BM&S) team. The researchers of BM&S Group are devoted to elucidating the fundamental science and performance of sustainable building materials. The research mainly focuses on developing green concrete utilizing various wastes through experimentation and implementing AI&ML. Before Joining as an assistant professor, she worked as a research scientist at Shriram Institute for Industrial Research, New Delhi, and developed a strength prediction model for hardened mortar by determining reactive silica content.

entries.title: Dr. Namrata Sathe
entries.excerpt: Dr. Namrata Sathe is an Assistant Professor at the Dadasaheb Phalke International Film Institute, MIT-WPU. She holds a PhD in Media Studies from Southern Illinois University, Carbondale. Her book, The Neoliberal Self in Bollywood: Cinema, Popular Culture, and Identity (Intellect Books, 2024), examines the consequences of unbridled expansion of neoliberal values within India through the lens of popular film and culture. Her scholarship has been published in prominent academic journals including Jump Cut, Journal of Cinema and Media Studies, and New Review of Film and Television Studies , as well as in edited collections on Indian cinema and media. She serves as the Assistant Editor for the SCOPUS-listed academic journal Studies in South Asian Film and Media . Her research interests include feminist media studies, queer studies, sexuality studies, and popular culture.

entries.text: Dr. Nathrao Bhimrao Jadhav Assistant Professor and Assistant Director, Admissions Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Photonic Integrated Circuit, Unmanned Aerial Vehicle, Image Processing, Optical Character Recognition. Nathrao B. Jadhav is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering with 17 years of teaching experience. He has an impressive academic background with a Bachelor's degree in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering, a Master's degree in Digital Electronics and Advanced Communication Systems, and a Ph.D. in Electronics and Communication Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University. His research interests lie in the field of Photonic Integrated Circuit, Unmanned Aerial Vehicle, Image Processing, and Optical Character Recognition. Nathrao has published seven research papers and received one patent grant for his innovative work in these areas. With his extensive knowledge and expertise, he is highly regarded by his colleagues and students alike. Nathrao is also actively involved in various academic and research activities and is always on the lookout for opportunities to contribute to the advancement of technology in his field.

entries.title: Dr. Neha Bokey
entries.excerpt: Human gut microbiome, metagenomics and metabolomics, molecular biology, biochemistry, and phylogenetic analysis.
entries.text: Dr. Neha Bokey Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Human gut microbiome, metagenomics and metabolomics, molecular biology, biochemistry, and phylogenetic analysis. Dr. Neha Bokey is Assistant Professor in the School of Biosciences and Technology, She completed her doctorate degree from Department of Biotechnology SGBAU Amravati University. Her Ph.D. topic was DNA barcoding and phylogenetic analysis of Dioscorea species. She also completed 6 Weeks Technology Entrepreneurship Development Program (TEDP) sponsored by Department of Science and Technology (DST) conducted by IITM HTIC MedTech Incubator virtually in 2021. She completed NIDHI-EIR fellowship from HTIC med tech incubator IITM in 2023. During her fellowship period she was working on gutmicrobiome study for better health. Her research interest include, human gut microbiome (with focus on role of microbiome in pathological metabolic conditions like, IBS and IBD) and molecular biology. Since 2021, she has been actively working on comparative analysis metabolomics and metagenomics of gut microbiome. Her research group is working on how gut health is related to different dietary pattern and health parameters.

entries.title: Dr. Nikhilesh Joardar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Nikhilesh Joardar, M.Sc., Ph.D. is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering at Dr. Vishwanath D. Karad MIT-World Peace University, Pune. His research spans parasitology, host–pathogen interactions, immunology, and antiparasitic drug development. Dr. Joardar previously worked as a Postdoctoral Research Associate at the Department of Immunology & Microbiology, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and Department of Medicine (Division of Infectious Diseases) Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis, contributing to studies on bacterial, parasitic infections and biomarker discovery. He did his PhD on antifilarial drug development and host-pathogen interaction from the Department of Zoology, Visva Bharati (An Institute of national importance). He has published extensively in leading journals, received multiple national research fellowships, and serves as an Associate Editor for Frontiers in Neglected Tropical Diseases and review editor in Frontiers in Parasitic Diseases. He is a life member of National Environmental Science Academy (NESA), New Delhi and member of American Society for Tropical Medicine and Hygiene (ASTMH). His research interest is focused on exploring redox biology for novel therapeutic interventions in parasitic and infectious diseases and host-pathogen interaction.

entries.title: Dr. Nilam Mahesh Pradhan
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Industrial Internet of Things, Networking
entries.text: Dr. Nilam Mahesh Pradhan Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Industrial Internet of Things, Networking Prof. Nilam M. Pradhan is a highly qualified and experienced educator with a passion for research in the field of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering. She completed her Bachelor's degree in Electronics Engineering from Shivaji University in 2002, followed by a Master's degree in VLSI & Embedded System from Pune University in 2012. Currently pursuing her Ph.D. degree with the Maharashtra Institute of Technology, Pune, her research interests lie in the area of Internet of Things and wireless sensor networks. As a member of IEEE, SAE, and a Lifetime Member of ISTE, Prof. Pradhan has published papers in both national and international journals and conferences. With over 12 years of teaching experience, she has taught a wide range of subjects such as Computer Network, Digital Electronics, Object Oriented Programming, and Analog Communication, among others.

entries.title: Dr. Nilesh Bajad
entries.excerpt: Dr. Nilesh Bajad completed his Doctoral degree from the Indian Institute of Technology (BHU), Varanasi, under the Prime Minister’s Research Fellowship (PMRF), India’s most prestigious doctoral fellowship awarded by the Ministry of Education, Government of India. He earned his Master’s degree from IIT (BHU), where he developed a strong foundation in Pharmaceutical Chemistry, and completed his B Pharm from the Government College of Pharmacy, Amravati. He is an active Member of the Royal Society of Chemistry (MRSC) (2025–Present). He has qualified several national-level competitive examinations, including GPAT (AIR 260), GATE (Life Sciences), GAT-B/JNU-CEEB (AIR 29), and NIPER-JEE in 2019.

entries.title: Dr. Nithya N Kutty
entries.excerpt: Plant specialised metabolism, plant–microbe interactions, plant volatiles, metabolomics, and green chemistry.
entries.text: Dr. Nithya N Kutty Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Plant specialised metabolism, plant–microbe interactions, plant volatiles, metabolomics, and green chemistry. Dr. Nithya N. Kutty has completed her doctoral study from IIT Kharagpur. Her doctoral study was focused on dynamics of scent volatiles synthesis, storage and emission in flowers of tuberose, a widely cultivated ornamental plant in Asian countries. During her doctoral study (through CSIR JRF/SRF fellowship), she has also carried out collaborative research work in the area of metabolite profiling from plant endophytes, hairy roots of carrots and studied the effect of gamma rays on floral volatiles. She has also presented her research work in several national and international conferences. She pursued her post-graduation (M Tech) in Agricultural biotechnology from IIT Kharagpur and completed her graduation (B Tech Biotechnology) from JNTU Hyderabad. Her research area of interests includes plant specialized metabolism, plant-microbe interactions, plant volatiles and metabolomics. Presently she is teaching basic and applied courses at undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral levels. She is a recognized PhD supervisor of the university. She also mentors students for their research project. Recently, she could secure "DST Young scientist and Technologist" grant under which she is working on vaporiz

entries.title: Dr. Omkar Kamalakant Kulkarni
entries.excerpt: Prof. Omkar Kulkarni is an Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. With 9 years of experience in education, teaching, and research, he has been actively involved in undergraduate and postgraduate teaching, as well as departmental and university-level activities. He recently earned his PhD in "Investigations on Micro-Forming of Bio-Materials" from MIT World Peace University and has published over 25 papers in optimization, algorithms, and micro-forming. He has also organized and participated in various workshops, conferences, and training programs. Research Area Cohort intelligence, Optimization, Nature inspired algorithms, Scocio inspired algorithms Publication 1) Novel product design of tool for investigating formability with microstructural study of bio-material titanium grade-II thin foils 2) Formability Assessment with Microstructural Investigations for Zirconium 702 Thin Foils: Bio-Material Applications. 3) Experimental and numerical investigations on forming limit curves in micro forming 4) Optimization of Railway Bogie Snubber Spring with Grasshopper Algorithm 5) Cuckoo Search Optimization- A Review Google Scholar Link https://scholar.google.co.in/citations?user=QvKqIyQAAAAJ&hl=en Scopus Link https://www.scopus.com/authid/detail.uri?authorId=56743026100 Orcid Link https://orcid.org/0000-0003-2375-959X

entries.excerpt: Adsorption, membrane separation, polymer composite, green composite, waste management, sustainable processes and renewable energy
entries.text: Dr. Pallavi Prashant Tatpate Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering Adsorption, membrane separation, polymer composite, green composite, waste management, sustainable processes and renewable energy Dr. Pallavi Tatpate is an experienced academician and researcher with a background in Chemical and Polymer Engineering. She has more than 10 years of academic experience and 1 year of industrial experience. She teaches courses such as Momentum Transfer, Process Heat Transfer, Mass Transfer, Process Calculations, and Process Instrumentation. Dr. Tatpate’s research work is focused on the membrane formation, characterization and application for wastewater and effluent treatment. Also her research interests include adsorption, membrane separation, polymer composite, green composite, waste management, sustainable processes, and renewable energy.

entries.title: Dr. Parveen Kumar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Parveen Kumar is a passionate teacher and researcher with over one decade of experience. He holds PhD in Wastewater Management from prestigious Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee (IITR). He has qualified NET examination in 2005 and 2015. He has supervised 2 students for PhD work in area of Agro-waste Management and Wastewater Management. He has authored 25 research papers and 3 book chapters in various referred journals and conferences. He has given presentations in various national and international conferences and has been resource person in webinars. He has made professional visit to Malaysia. Dr. Kumar has worked with GD Goenka University and Amity University Haryana before joining as Assistant Professor at School of Business, MIT WPU. Research Areas: Circular Economy, Sustainable Waste Management, CSR and Sustainability, Water Resources Management

entries.title: Dr. Poonam Inamdar
entries.excerpt: Bioinorganic Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Molecular Modelling
entries.text: Dr. Poonam Inamdar Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Bioinorganic Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, Molecular Modelling Dr. Poonam R. Inamdar works in the field of synthetic inorganic chemistry. Her research area interests are the synthesis of the metal complexes and evaluation of their biological studies and biomolecular interactions. She is also working on projects which include protein interaction studies of the organic ligands for the design of potent pharmacophores using spectrometric techniques. Her research focus is mainly an exploration of biomolecular interaction studies of the compounds using UV, FTIR, Conductometry, Cyclic voltammetry, Fluorescence spectroscopy, Viscosity and Circular dichroism studies as well as gel electrophoresis studies of the metal complexes for the assessment of DNA cleavage studies.

entries.title: Dr. Prajakta Vishal Adsule
entries.excerpt: Synthetic Chemistry,Pharmaceutical Organic Chemistry,Computer Aided Drug Design,Green Chemistry,Microwave Synthesis
entries.text: Dr. Prajakta Vishal Adsule Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Synthetic Chemistry,Pharmaceutical Organic Chemistry,Computer Aided Drug Design,Green Chemistry,Microwave Synthesis Dr. Prajakta Adsule is an accomplished academician and researcher in the field of Pharmaceutical Chemistry. She graduated with a First Class degree in Pharmacy from Shivaji University, Kolhapur in 2003, and subsequently completed her M.Pharm and Ph.D. degrees with First Class from Shivaji University Kolhapur and Savitribai Phule Pune University respectively. Dr. Adsule has 16 years of experience in teaching and 6 years of research experience. She is currently serving as an Assistant Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, School of Pharmacy, Pune. Dr. Adsule has published numerous research papers in national and international journals and is a life member of APTI and IPA. She has also received research grants and awards for her contributions to the field.

entries.title: Dr. Prajkta Bhide
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prajkta Bhide is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Public Health at MIT-WPU. She completed her Masters in Health Science degree from Savitribai Phule Pune University (formerly known as University of Pune). She holds a doctoral degree (PhD in Health Science) from Savitribai Phule Pune University. Her doctoral work explored the epidemiology of birth defects / congenital disorders in India and their larger public health impact. She areas of expertise include conducting systematic reviews and meta-analyses, and quantitative data analyses. Her areas of interest include birth defects, maternal and child health, epidemiology and health economics.

He is currently pursuing a PhD from the same university. He completed his postgraduation in Manufacturing Technology from NIT Trichy in Tamilnadu, India and his graduation in Mechanical Engineering from Government College of Engineering Aurangabad in Maharashtra, India. Prof. Pesode specializes in Material Science, Engineering Metallurgy, Advanced Manufacturing Processes, Biomaterials, Surface modification of biomaterials, Biomaterial coating, Composites and Nanocomposites for biomedical applications. He is a life member of several professional bodies, including the Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE) and the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE). Prof. Pesode has published more than 19 research papers in various international journals and conferences and has worked for numerous conferences, seminars, and educational workshops. entries.title: Dr. PrasantaKumar Sudarshan Ghosh
entries.excerpt: Organic and inorganic functional nanomaterials, organic field-effect transistors, hybrid materials, chemical sensors, and research instrumentation.
entries.text: Dr. PrasantaKumar Sudarshan Ghosh Assistant Professor Department of Physics Organic and inorganic functional nanomaterials, organic field-effect transistors, hybrid materials, chemical sensors, and research instrumentation. Dr. Prasanta Ghosh is an applied physicist with 16 years of research experience and 9 years of teaching experience. He conducts research in organic/inorganic functional nanomaterials, including organic Field Effect Transistors, inorganic/organic hybrids, and advanced sensors. Dr. Ghosh has published 47 research papers in SCI journals, conferences, and books, and has been granted 3 patents by South African Innovation Patent. He is an advisory board member of Materials Research Express and Nanotechnology, both peer-reviewed SCOPUS indexed journals. Dr. Ghosh has also received several awards, including the Top Peer Reviewer in Materials Science by Publons. Apart from his scientific work, he has also developed indigenous instruments and provides low-cost automation and instrumentation solutions for research and industry.
