entries.excerpt: BTMS, CFD, IC Engines, Combustion, Alternate Fuels
entries.text: Dr. Prashant Mahadeo Patane Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering BTMS, CFD, IC Engines, Combustion, Alternate Fuels Prof. Prashant Patane is an accomplished academician and mechanical engineer, currently serving as an Assistant Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. With a solid background in Thermal Engineering and extensive experience in Engine Simulation software such as GT-Power, Matlab-Simulink & CFD, he plays a vital role in shaping the minds of the future generation of engineers. He completed his M.Tech in Thermal Engg. with CGPA 8.11 from the College of Engg. Pune, and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from the College of Engineering, Pune. Prior to his academic career, he worked with SKF India Ltd, Pune.

entries.title: Dr. Pratiksha Deepak Khurpade
entries.excerpt: Nanomaterials, Catalysis, Biofuels, Flow through Porous Media, Energy
entries.text: Dr. Pratiksha Deepak Khurpade Assistant Professor Department of Chemical Engineering Department of Materials Science & Engineering Nanomaterials, Catalysis, Biofuels, Flow through Porous Media, Energy Dr. Pratiksha Khurpade is an experienced academician and researcher with a background in Chemical and Petroleum Engineering. She holds an M. Tech in Chemical Engineering from LIT Nagpur and a Ph. D in Petroleum Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University. With over 14 years of academic experience and 2 years of industrial experience, she teaches courses such as Momentum Transfer, Process Heat Transfer, Process Instrumentation at the undergraduate level, and Catalysis Technology for Masters level students. Dr. Khurpade's previous research work focused on the synthesis of biodiesel from various feedstocks and its blending with petroleum diesel, along with the prediction of properties of diesel-biodiesel blends. Her present research interests include flow through porous media, nanomaterial synthesis and its applications, and green corrosion inhibitors.

entries.title: Dr. Preethi Sunkara
entries.excerpt: Plant developmental biology, plant–pathogen interactions, combined biotic and abiotic stress, plant genetics, and molecular biology.
entries.text: Dr. Preethi Sunkara Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Plant developmental biology, plant–pathogen interactions, combined biotic and abiotic stress, plant genetics, and molecular biology. Dr. Preethi Sunkara is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU. She is a Molecular and Developmental Plant Biologist who earned her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore. Her doctoral research focused on the biochemical and genetic characterization of TCP3 and TCP4 transcription factors in Arabidopsis thaliana, specifically investigating their antagonistic roles in leaf development and growth. Her expertise spans advanced plant genetics, including EMS mutagenesis, ChIP assays, and confocal microscopy, Protein interactions, Molecular biology. An accomplished researcher, Preethi has published in journals such as PLoS Genetics and Plant Physiology. She received the second prize for best poster at the 29th International Conference on Arabidopsis Research (ICAR-2018) in Finland. Beyond her primary research, she has supervised numerous Masters and undergraduate student projects and possesses a strong background in biotechnology and Plant developmental biology. Her teaching interests include Molecular Biology, Genetic Engineering, Genetics and applied Biotechnology .

entries.excerpt: Semiconductor nanostructures, two-dimensional materials, thin films, sensors, and optoelectronic devices.
entries.text: Dr. Rahul Ashokrao Aher Assistant Professor Department of Physics Semiconductor nanostructures, two-dimensional materials, thin films, sensors, and optoelectronic devices. Dr. Rahul A. Aher obtained his Ph.D. in Advanced 2D Materials and their applications from Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune. He also holds a Master's degree from the same university and is qualified in SET and GATE. During his Ph.D. tenure, he received the Bharatratn J.R.D.Tata Gunwant Sanshodhak Vidyarthi Award. He previously served as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Physics at Fergusson College, Pune. His research interests include Advanced 2D Materials, Quantum Materials, Opto-magneto-electronics, Field emission, Electrochemistry, Photodetector, and Photo-catalysis materials. He has published more than 25 research articles in reputed indexed journals.

entries.title: Dr. Rahul Verma
entries.excerpt: Classical Sociological Theories, Sociology of Religion, Social Networks, Sociology of Industry
entries.text: Dr. Rahul Verma Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Classical Sociological Theories, Sociology of Religion, Social Networks, Sociology of Industry Dr. Rahul Verma is a trained and qualified sociologist with a passion for Pedagogy and Social Research. He possesses an in-depth understanding and interpretation of empirical reality, which has been acquired through the collection and analysis of primary data from various themes such as the use of Social Media, Social Change and Modernity, Religious Pluralism, and Inter-Religious Interaction. Driven by a natural curiosity towards investigating social phenomena, he employs both Qualitative and Quantitative research methods. Research Area Classical Sociological Theories, Sociology of Religion, Social Networks, Sociology of Industry Publication Published a research paper titled “The Prevalence of Religious Pluralism and Inter-Faith Dialogue in India: An Empirical Comparison of Hindus and Muslims of Bhopal” in Panjab University Research Journal (Arts), January-June Issue 2019, ISSN: 0970-5260, pp – 46-67. (UGC-CARE Approved) Orchid Link https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7413-0244

entries.title: Dr. Raj Agarwal
entries.excerpt: Dr. Raj Agarwal, Program Coordinator of the M.Tech in Medical Devices and Assistant Professor in the Department of Bioengineering, School of Engineering & Technology at MITWPU, is an accomplished researcher and innovator. He holds Master of Technology in Biomechanical Engineering and a PhD in 3D printing of bone screws from Thapar Institute of Engineering & Technology, Patiala. With over 3 years of Teaching and 4.5 years of research experience, his expertise is in 3D Printing, Biomaterials, Polymer matrix composites, Tissue Engineering, Metal matrix composites, Machine Learning, Patient-Specific Implants and Medical Devices. His teaching philosophy emphasizes interdisciplinary learning, ethical responsibility, and innovation-driven research contributes to MITWPU’s vision of shaping socially responsible leaders and innovators who bridge academia, industry, and healthcare for transformative impact.

He completed his ME(Electronics) in 2005 and is currently pursuing his PhD from SPPU Pune. entries.title: Dr. Raju Shivaji Ingale
entries.excerpt: Metal-oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing.
entries.text: Dr. Raju Shivaji Ingale Assistant Professor Department of Physics Metal-oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing. Dr. Raju Shivaji Ingale has over 15 years of experience as an academician, including 11 years as an Assistant Professor of Physics at MIT-WPU, Pune. He holds a Ph.D. in Nanotechnology from the Department of Physics, PSGVPM ACS College Shahada, Kavayitri Bahinabai Chaudhari North Maharashtra University, Jalgaon, and M.Sc. in Physics from prestigious Fergusson College Pune, affiliated with Savitribai Phule Pune University. Dr. Ingale has qualified both the SET and GATE examinations established himself as a dedicated researcher and academician. His expertise lies in the fabrication, characterization, and design of cost-effective doped metal oxide and nanocomposite thick and thin films for gas sensing applications. He has authored 7 international research papers published in reputed journals and holds two granted international patents underscoring his innovative approach to research. Beyond academia, he is actively involved in creating impactful technological solutions, such as a women’s safety device, showcasing his commitment to addressing societal challenges through innovation.

The Study involved Molecular Docking of plant-based compounds against cancer related targets; Molecular Dynamic Simulation of complexes and Pathway Simulations. He has experience in NGS Sequence analysis in both- industry and academic settings, including, NIPL (Nucleome Informatics Pvt. Ltd.), Hyderabad; ICAR-NIHSAD (National Institute of High Security Animal Diseases), Bhopal; SCMS (Department of Scientific Computing Modeling and Simulations), Savitribai Phule Pune University; BRIC-THSTI (Translational Health Science and Technology Institute), Faridabad. He has worked on Whole Genome Analysis, Whole Transcriptome Analysis, and Metagenome Analysis of data generated using pacbio, nanopore and illumina platforms.

entries.text: Dr. Samidha Anand Jawade Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Material Science, Additive manufacturing, Engineering Metallurgy Dr. Samidha Jawade is an accomplished mechanical engineer and researcher with a specialization in Additive Manufacturing. She has a PhD in Mechanical Engineering and several years of experience in the fields of Material Science, Additive Manufacturing, and Engineering Metallurgy. Dr. Jawade currently serves as an Assistant Professor at MIT-WPU, where she is responsible for teaching and mentoring students in mechanical engineering and related disciplines. As a researcher, she has published several research papers and has been actively involved in various projects exploring the use of additive manufacturing technologies to produce novel materials and structures with unique properties. Dr. Jawade is passionate about advancing the field of mechanical engineering and material science through research, teaching, and industry collaborations.

entries.title: Dr. Sandeep Namdev Dhikale
entries.excerpt: Dr. Sandeep Dhikale, also known as Sandeepsagar, embodies a distinctive confluence of the traditional Gurukul system and modern educational paradigms. His academic pursuits span a spectrum of disciplines, including Sanskrit, Peace Studies, Indology, and Meditation. Dr. Sandeep has actively contributed to academia by presenting over 22 research papers at various international, national, and state-level conferences, reflecting his interdisciplinary approach and commitment to scholarly excellence. He is also the author of three acclaimed books: ‘Understanding Peace’; ‘Dimensions of Peace’; ‘Indian Knowledge Systems: A Treasure Trove of the Way of Holistic and Sustainable Life’ These works reflect his deep engagement with the themes of peace, holistic living, and the rich heritage of Indian knowledge traditions. He is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in Peace Studies at the School of Business and Management, MIT World Peace University, Pune.

entries.title: Dr. Savitri Nathrao Jadhav
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Savitri N. Jadhav is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering with over 18+ years of academic and research experience. She completed her Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2022. Handwritten MODI character recognition was her topic of research. Her research interests span Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning (AIML), Embedded AI, Image Processing, Pattern Recognition, and Edge AI. She has contributed extensively to interdisciplinary domains including UAV-based flood detection, anomaly detection using embedded AI, and intelligent agricultural systems. She has published numerous research papers in reputed journals and conferences and holds multiple Indian patents in areas such as crop damage assessment, non-invasive glucose monitoring, and UV-C disinfection systems. She is actively involved in consultancy projects with industry partners like KPIT Technologies and Bharat Flow Analytics, focusing on real-world AI and embedded system applications. A professional member of IEEE and associated with the Geoscience and Remote Sensing Society, she is committed to advancing research, innovation, and student mentorship in emerging technologies.

entries.title: Dr. Shalaka Patil
entries.excerpt: Genome stability in cancer, DNA damage and repair, cancer epigenetics, phase separation, and optogenetic tools.
entries.text: Dr. Shalaka Patil Assistant Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Genome stability in cancer, DNA damage and repair, cancer epigenetics, phase separation, and optogenetic tools. Dr. Shalaka Patil is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Technology, MIT-WPU, Pune. She is a dedicated and passionate researcher with a PhD in Biology from the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER), Pune, under the supervision of Prof. Kundan Sengupta. She holds university rank 1 for her M.Sc. in microbiology from SPPU. She has qualified CSIR-UGC NET-JRF with AIR 22. Her research area is genome biology and chromosomal instability. Her doctoral work focused on understanding the role of the nuclear envelope in maintaining the genomic stability of cancer cells. After her PhD, she worked as a Senior Project Associate at IISER Pune on a project to develop a cost-effective FISH kit for detecting HER2 amplification in breast cancer patient samples. Later, she joined the CNRS Institute of Human Genetics, Montpellier, France, for her postdoctoral research with Dr. Marcel Mechali. Her post-doctoral work was focused on developing an in vivo system to examine the phase separation of the initiation of DNA replication. Through her work, she aims to contribute to advancements in medical research, particularly developing strategies for the early diagnosis of can

entries.title: Dr. Sharbani Kaushik
entries.excerpt: Biomaterials, Bioenergy, Electrochemical devices, Biosensors, Microbial Electrolysis, CO2 conversion
entries.text: Dr. Sharbani Kaushik Program Director Department of Bioengineering Biomaterials, Bioenergy, Electrochemical devices, Biosensors, Microbial Electrolysis, CO2 conversion Dr. Sharbani Kaushik is a renowned biotechnology researcher with expertise in bioenergy, bioelectronics, and membrane proteins. She completed her Ph.D. in the School of Energy Science and Engineering from IIT Guwahati. Her work in renewable energy and bioanalytical applications includes pioneering research on microbial fuel cells and biofuel cell biosensing devices. As a postdoctoral researcher at The Ohio State University, USA, she made significant contributions to chemistry and biochemistry, especially in membrane proteins, synthetic biology and drug discovery, resulting in multiple high-impact publications. She has also served as an Assistant Professor at the Regional Institute of Science and Technology in Meghalaya, where she introduced new courses and mentored students in biosensors and biomedical instrumentation.

She has doctorate degree in Microbiology. entries.title: Dr. Shital Vasant Kahane
entries.excerpt: Metal–semiconductor quantum dots and nanomaterials for optoelectronic, energy-storage, and biological applications.
entries.text: Dr. Shital Vasant Kahane Assistant Professor Department of Physics Metal–semiconductor quantum dots and nanomaterials for optoelectronic, energy-storage, and biological applications. Dr. Shital Kahane, a Ph.D. holder in Physics with a focus on nanomaterials from Pune University, has been teaching for 5 years and currently holds the position of Assistant Professor at the Department of Physics in MIT-WPU, Kothrud, Pune. Her recent studies have been centered around the synthesis of metal-semiconductor quantum dots/nanomaterials, with applications ranging from liquid crystal displays, supercapacitors, to biological applications. Research Area Metal-semiconductor quantum dots, Applications Publication 1. CdO-CdS nanocomposites with enhanced photocatalytic activity for hydrogen generation. 2. A study of charge transfer mechanism and optical properties of Au-CdS core-shell nanocrystals. 3. Anomalous photoluminescence enhancement due to hot electron transfer in core shell Au-CdS nanocrystals Google Scholar Link https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Ec6ychUAAAAJ&hl=en&authuser=1 Scopus Link https://www.scopus.com/search/form.uri?display=basic#author Orcid Link https://orcid.org/my-orcid?orcid=0000-0002-9556-1868

entries.title: Dr. Shraddha Kulkarni
entries.excerpt: Dr. Shraddha Kulkarni is a passionate academician with a wealth of 17 years of experience in engineering teaching and research in Biotechnology. Her experience includes teaching interdisciplinary and application oriented courses like Fermentation Technology, Bioprocess Engineering, Biothermodynamics, Bioseparation Engineering, Bioreaction Engineering etc. She has always emphasized on experiential learning with hands-on training of the students to make them industry ready. she has worked for development of syllabus for B.Tech Biotechnology, NAAC and NBA accreditation of the programs, development of laboratory courses etc. Beyond the classroom, Dr. Kulkarni is deeply engaged in cutting-edge research endeavors. She has completed her Ph.D from Department of Technology, SPPU Pune. Her areas of expertise span across diverse domains within Bioengineering, encompassing fermentation technology, Bioprocess Design, proteomic technology, computational studies of biomolecules. Through her research, she endeavors to address the development of environmentally friendly surfactant products and their application in various fields. Committed to the advancement of her field, Dr. Kulkarni is an author, with numerous publications in esteemed journals and conferences. She has fetched research grants from agencies like Savitribai Phule Pune University, Institution of Engineers, etc. As an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Dr. Kulkarni embodies the institution's ethos of promoting peace through education and research. Her unwavering commitment to excellence, coupled with her passion for fostering innovation, makes her a valued member of the academic community, dedicated to shaping the engineers of tomorrow and contributing to a brighter, more sustainable world.

entries.title: Dr. Shreya Bera
entries.excerpt: Dr. Shreya Bera is an Assistant Professor of English at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. She completed her PhD in Literary Studies from the University of Szeged in January 2023 under the prestigious Stipendium Hungaricum Scholarship Programme (2018-2023). She attended multiple international conferences such as the 14th Biennial Conference of the Hungarian Society for the Study of English (HUSSE) in Hungary (2019), 11th Brno International Conference, Czech Republic, ESSE Doctoral Symposium in France (2020), Narratives and Self Ambivalence symposium, SAS, London in 2021 and more. She also co-edited a book titled Critical Essays on Multicultural India: Exploring Inclusiveness and New Humanities published in 2017. She has also authored articles and book reviews in peer-reviewed, and Scopus indexed journals such as Americana e-journal, British and American studies (B.A.S.) journal, The ESSE Messenger etc. She is an established author of two books published in the years of 2017 and 2025.

entries.title: Dr. Shubhangi Rastogi
entries.excerpt: Bioethanol, Biocatalyst, lignocellulosic residues, probiotics, and yogurts.
entries.text: Dr. Shubhangi Rastogi Assistant Professor Department of Bioengineering Bioethanol, Biocatalyst, lignocellulosic residues, probiotics, and yogurts. Dr. Shubhangi Rastogi is an experienced academician and researcher with a background in Microbiology and Industrial Biotechnology. She holds a Master of Science in Biotechnology and a Ph. D. in Microbiology from Panjab University, Chandigarh. With over 2 years of industrial and 1.5 years of research experience, her expertise is in bioprocess: biocatalyst, microbial cultures, 2-G bio-ethanol production, probiotic development, and in-vitro drug development. She teaches bioprocess engineering and industrial biotechnology at the undergraduate level. Dr. Rastogi has been awarded with a DBT-BIRAC E-YUVA scale-up project on bioethanol production in 2022. Dr. Rastogi’s previous research focused on the scale-up of biocatalyst cocktail production and utilization in the biological pretreatment of lignocellulosic residues for bioethanol production. Her present research interests include the scale-up of biocatalysts, specifically ligno-hemicellulases, biological pretreatment of lignocelluloses with a major focus on rice straw, bioethanol production, and development of probiotics and yogurts for exploring gut gut-brain axis.

entries.title: Dr. Soubir Das
entries.excerpt: Dr. Soubir Das holds a Ph.D. in Petroleum Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad, specializing in the development of clay-free drilling fluid systems for gas hydrate-bearing formations. His research focused on formulating low-dosage hydrate inhibitors (LDHIs) for water-based, non-damaging polymeric drilling fluids, ensuring enhanced performance and minimal reservoir damage. With expertise in drilling technology, drilling fluids, and LDHI synthesis, Dr. Das has extensive experience with advanced analytical instruments such as FT-IR, TGA-DTA-DSC, Particle Size Analyzer, Zeta Potential Analyzer, and high- pressure dynamic flow-loop systems (up to 10,000 psi). He holds an MTech. Petroleum Engineering and B.E. Petroleum and Offshore Engineering from IIT(ISM) Dhanbad and AMET University Chennai as well. He has collaborated with the Gas Hydrate Research & Technology Centre (GHRTC), ONGC, Panvel, Navi Mumbai applying theoretical insights to industry challenges during his Ph.D. Dr. Das has authored multiple SCI-indexed journal publications and presented at national and international conferences. He is skilled in both independent research and multidisciplinary teamwork, contributing to advancements in petroleum engineering.

entries.title: Dr. Suchitra Renguntwar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Suchitra Renguntwar (Ajgaonkar) is an Assistant Professor of Psychology in the Department of Liberal Arts at MIT WPU, teaching industrial psychology, psychological assessment in HRD and counselling, and health psychology. She holds a Ph.D. in management and a master’s degree in psychology and is qualified for UGC NET and SET in psychology. She has over six years of experience teaching MBA students and eight years of industry experience. Her empirical research focuses on workforce agility in the information technology industry, learning mechanisms and TQM in the manufacturing industry, and resilience and women empowerment in social enterprises. Her research papers are published in ABDC (B) category journals – International Journal of Organizational Analysis and TQM Journal. Her research interests include socio-cognitive elements in psychology, nudge theory, and collaborative research on the application of psychology to management. She has expertise in mixed-method research, systematic literature review, and qualitative research methods.

entries.title: Dr. Suman Rawat
entries.department: Department of Wellness and Yogic Science
entries.excerpt: Dr. Suman Rawat is serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Wellness and Yogic Science at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, MIT-WPU. She is passionate about promoting holistic health and wellness through yoga education and practices. With experience in teaching yogic philosophy and therapeutic applications of yoga, she brings both academic depth and practical insight to her role. Dr. Rawat holds a Ph.D. in Yoga from Shoolini University and has qualified for the UGC-NET (Yoga). Her academic qualifications also include an M.Sc. in Yoga Therapy from Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) and a B.Sc. in Yoga Science from Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga (MDNIY). She has also published research papers in the field of yoga and wellness. Before joining MIT-WPU, she was the Academic and NAAC Co-ordinator at Shoolini University.

entries.title: Dr. Sushila Manish Palwe
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Computer Vision, Machine Learning, Databases
entries.text: Dr. Sushila Manish Palwe Assistant Professor Department of Computer Engineering and Technology Computer Vision, Machine Learning, Databases With over 19 years of experience in Academics and Teaching, Dr. Sushila Palwe specializes in teaching Databases, Machine Learning, Data Science, Data Mining, and Business Intelligence. She has developed innovative teaching methodologies to enhance the learning process, benefiting numerous students. Dr. Palwe has received grants for research projects and international workshops from SPPU BUCD. She has mentored and guided undergraduate and post-graduate students as well as PhD candidates, with over 55 research publications and book chapters to her credit. She has authored three books and organized several seminars, workshops, and FDPs. Dr. Palwe has also played a leading role in academic responsibilities, such as NAAC Criteria-1, NBA Criteria-II, ISO, and Infosys Campus Connect Program InCharge, among others. Dr. Palwe currently serves as the B Tech CSE(AI-DS) Programmer Coordinator and is involved in overall curriculum development for B Tech CSE(AI-DS). Dr. Palwe has also contributed to various consultancy projects sponsored by companies such as Blackberry, Whirlpool, and Idiada.

entries.title: Dr. Sushrut Hemant Vinchurkar

He received his Doctorate in Philosophy with specialization in Water Resources Engineering from Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology, Surat. With an overall experience of 8 years, including 4 years in academics and 4 years in research, Dr. Vinchurkar has published 4 research papers in international journals and presented over 15 articles at various national and international conferences. In his free time, he enjoys writing blogs on case-studies related to the field of Water Resources Engineering.

entries.text: Dr. Swetha Rayala Assistant Professor Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences Drug design, discovery against Neurodegenerative disorders. Computational design, molecular docking, molecular dynamics simulations, QSAR modelling. AI and ML based screening and design of small molecules. Synthetic medicinal chemistry, in vitro, in vivo assays, solubility enhancement for poorly soluble drugs Dr. Rayala Swetha is a medicinal chemist specializing in drug discovery for neurodegenerative diseases, with a particular focus on Alzheimer’s disease. Her research integrates computational design, chemical synthesis, and biological evaluation across enzyme assays, cell-based systems, and animal models to identify and design novel therapeutic molecules.

entries.title: Dr. Tanya Kumar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Tanya Kumar is an Assistant Professor at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, where she is recognized for her dynamic teaching approach and strong research orientation. With over four years of academic experience in marketing and finance, she brings conceptual clarity, industry relevance, and learner-centric pedagogy into the classroom. Her teaching interests span Marketing Management, Strategic Management, and Consumer Behaviour, with a focus on developing analytical thinking and practical decision-making skills among students. Beyond teaching, she plays an active role in academic administration and institutional development, contributing to curriculum design, NAAC documentation, and the coordination of Faculty Development Programmes (FDPs). She is a prolific researcher with publications in reputed Scopus-, Web of Science-, and ABDC-listed journals, and has authored several international book chapters with globally renowned publishers including IGI Global, Emerald, Routledge, and Wiley. Driven by a passion for academic excellence, leadership, and innovation, Dr. Tanya is committed to advancing impactful research and nurturing future-ready management professionals through quality education and scholarly engagement.

entries.title: Dr. Vaishali Namdevrao Choudhari
entries.excerpt: Psychology, Mental health
entries.text: Dr. Vaishali Namdevrao Choudhari Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Psychology, Mental health Dr. Vaishali Choudhari is a scholar with UGC NET and PhD in Psychology. Her research interests include workplace conflict, positive psychology, mental health, and gender differences. She has authored several articles in reputed journals such as the Indian Journal of Psychology and Education, International Journal of Indian Psychology, and Indian Journal of Natural Sciences. Dr. Choudhari's work includes a systematic review of prenatal mental health in India, a comparative investigation of personality traits and gender differences among management students, and a study on haptic memory in deaf adolescents. She is a dedicated researcher and has made significant contributions to the field of psychology. Research Areas Psychology, Mental health Publications Positivity at Work-Place: A Comparative Investigation & An Inevitable Work-Place Conflict: An Overview Google Scholar link: Google Scholar

Thesis title “CONSUMPTION PATTERN OF ELECTRICITY IN RURAL AND URBAN AREAS: A CASE STUDY OF SONBHADRA AND LUCKNOW DISTRICT OF UTTAR PRADESH”. Also qualified for Lectureship, UGC NET - Commerce, 2019.

entries.title: Dr. Vedashree Tushar Mali
entries.excerpt: Microfinance, Financial Inclusion, Corporate Finance
entries.text: Dr. Vedashree Tushar Mali Assistant Professor Department of Business Microfinance, Financial Inclusion, Corporate Finance Dr. Vedashree Mali is an Assistant Professor at the MIT-WPU School of Business (WPU-SOB) in Pune, India. She completed her Ph.D. in Finance and an MBA in Finance from Savitribai Phule Pune University. At WPU-SOB, she teaches courses such as Corporate Finance, Financial Modeling, and Corporate Valuation. Her research interests include Microfinance, Financial Inclusion, and Corporate Finance. With her expertise in these areas, she has contributed to multiple research publications and conference presentations. Dr. Vedashree has also participated in various workshops, seminars, and training programs related to Finance and Financial Modeling.

entries.title: Dr. Veena Pravin Kshirsagar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Veena Vishwanath Sangvikar is an Asst. Prof and Program Head for B.Sc. Computational Mathematics & Statistics, Dept. of Mathematics and Statistics at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. She holds a Ph.D. in Mathematics with M.Sc. and M.Phil. qualified degrees. Has an experience of more than 16 years in higher education teaching & more than 6 years of research experience. She is a Life Member of the Indian Mathematical Society. She has published five research papers in Numerical Analysis of Fractional PDE and eight reference books for UG under Pune University. Her current research interest is Applications of Machine Learning and Deep Learning.

entries.title: Dr. Vibha Utpal Patel
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: wireless communication, Digital communication, Neural Networks, Machine Learning, Data Science, Electromagnetics and Antennas, and Digital signal processing.
entries.text: Dr. Vibha Utpal Patel Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering wireless communication, Digital communication, Neural Networks, Machine Learning, Data Science, Electromagnetics and Antennas, and Digital signal processing. Dr. Vibha Patel received her Ph.D. in Electronics & Communication from Gujarat Technological University in 2023. With over 12 years of teaching experience, she has made notable contributions to her field. Her research interests span wireless communication, digital communication, neural networks, machine learning, data science, electromagnetics and antennas, and digital signal processing. Dr. Patel's work has resulted in the grant of one Australian patent and one South African patent in her name. She has worked on various noteworthy projects, such as the TDMA-based data logger for AWS, moisture sensor using patch antenna, clustering analysis on world health data, and job recommender system, among others.

entries.title: Dr. Vikas Jaywant Magar
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Dr. Vikas J Magar received his M.Sc (Computer Science) and Ph.D. from the School of Computational Sciences, Solapur University Solapur in the years 2011 and 2020 respectively. He is also qualified NET(UGC) and SET (Maharashtra) exam. He has 11 years of experience teaching MCA, M.Sc.(CS), BCA, B.Sc(CS) students. He is a faculty member of the Department of Computer Science MIT-WPU, Pune. His fields of research interest include Fuzzy logic, Artificial Intelligence, Algorithm Design Strategy, data structure, etc. He has published 12 research papers in various international journals and conference proceedings. He has attended many conferences, workshops, and seminars.

entries.title: Dr. Vikas Keshav Jadhav
entries.excerpt: Political Sociology, Social Exclusion/Marginalization and Politics of Recognition, Inclusion and Justice; Development Sociology, Critical Phenomenology; Critical Caste Studies; and Sociology of Caste, Tribe and Inter-subjectivity with special reference to Dalit, Adivasis and De-notified and Nomadic Tribes of India.
entries.text: Dr. Vikas Keshav Jadhav Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts Political Sociology, Social Exclusion/Marginalization and Politics of Recognition, Inclusion and Justice; Development Sociology, Critical Phenomenology; Critical Caste Studies; and Sociology of Caste, Tribe and Inter-subjectivity with special reference to Dalit, Adivasis and De-notified and Nomadic Tribes of India. Dr. Vikas is a Ph.D. in Sociology from the Centre for the Study of Social Systems, Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi. He is also a trained development sector professional from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai, having an experience of field as well as working with Public Sector Unit – NTPC Limited. He employs an interdisciplinary approach and mixed- method framework to study the politics of marginalization in India. His work engages in examining the social, political and historical factors that accentuate the processes of alterity construction, stigmatization, exclusion, and how different social groups negotiate and counter diverse structural constraints.

entries.title: Dr. Vinayak Sudhakar Wadgaonkar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Vinayak S. Wadgaonkar has completed his doctoral study from L.I.T. nagpur. His doctoral study was focused on Studies on Removal of Heavy Metals By Low Cost Adsorbents. During his doctoral study, he has also carried out collaborative research work in the area of waste water treatment and environmental engineering. He has also presented her research work in several national and international conferences. He pursued his post-graduation (M. E.) in Chemical Engineering from Shivaji University and completed his graduation (B.E) in Chemical Engineering from Amravati University, Amravati. His research area of interests includes natural gas engineering, enhanced oil recovery, flow assurance, waste water treatment, hydrogen production and environmental engineering. Presently he is teaching basic and applied courses at undergraduate, post graduate. He is also recognized Phd guide in Petroleum and Chemical Engineering. He also mentors students for their research project.

entries.title: Dr. Vinod Sartape
entries.excerpt: Migration and Diaspora, Social Exclusion, Anthropology of Caste, Religion, Social Movements
entries.text: Dr. Vinod Sartape Assistant Professor(Sociology) Department of Liberal Arts Migration and Diaspora, Social Exclusion, Anthropology of Caste, Religion, Social Movements Dr Vinod Sartape is Assistant Professor of Sociology in the Department of Liberal Arts, MIT-World Peace University, Pune. He has obtained a doctorate in Sociology and Social Anthropology from Central European University, Vienna, Austria. His research areas include Migration and Diaspora, Social Exclusion, Anthropology of Caste, Religion, Globalisation, and Social Movements in India and the Globe. Dr Vinod has an MPhil in Sociology from Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi and an MA in Social Work from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai. He is qualified the NET-JRF in Sociology. Prior to joining MIT-WPU, Vinod has taught Sociology for three years at the Babes-Bolyai University, Romania

entries.title: Dr. Vishvajeet N
entries.excerpt: Theoretical computer science; Quantum information; Randomness and computation; Algorithms and complexity
entries.text: Dr. Vishvajeet N Postdoctoral Researcher, CNRS / LaBRI Quantum Science & Technology Group Theoretical computer science; Quantum information; Randomness and computation; Algorithms and complexity Vishvajeet N is a theoretical computer scientist working at the intersection of algorithms, randomness, and quantum information. He received his Ph.D. from Rutgers University and is currently a postdoctoral researcher with the Quantum Information and Computation group at LaBRI.

entries.title: Dr. Vivek Patel
entries.excerpt: Crashworthiness, Impact analysis, Design, Non-linear analysis, FEA
entries.text: Dr. Vivek Patel Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Crashworthiness, Impact analysis, Design, Non-linear analysis, FEA An Assistant Professor in the School of Engineering and Technology, Dr. Vivek Patel's research interests center around nonlinear structural analysis with a particular focus on studying the impact behavior of tubular components in the automobile industry. He is an expert in modeling and simulation of engineering parts using software such as AutoCAD, LS-Dyna, Ansys, and Abaqus. In addition, he has hands-on experience with experimental set-ups such as UTM, Hardness tester, and Ultrasonic assisted casting process. With his technical competency and expertise, he is capable of confirming the statistics of experimental tests with accuracy.

entries.title: Dr. Waseem Khan
entries.excerpt: Dr. Waseem Khan is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the School of Business at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He has over six years of diverse experience across academia, research, and industry, having been associated with reputed institutions such as Aligarh Muslim University, Jamia Hamdard, GLA University (Mathura), and the Indian Institute of Management, Lucknow, as well as with ICICI Bank Ltd. He holds a master’s degree and a Ph.D. in Agricultural Economics and Business Management from Aligarh Muslim University and is also qualified in the ASRB-NET in both Agricultural Business Management and Agricultural Economics.
entries.text: Dr. Waseem Khan Assistant Professor Department of Business Dr. Waseem Khan is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the School of Business at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. entries.title: Garima Chaudhary
entries.excerpt: Garima Chaudhary is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Visual Arts at the School of Design, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU). She holds Bachelor’s, Master’s, and PhD degrees in Fine Arts from Aligarh Muslim University, reflecting a strong academic foundation. With over two years of experience in Visual Arts education, Garima combines academic rigor with practical expertise. Her professional skills encompass research, drawing, painting, branding, print and digital media, semiotics, and photography, with a particular research interest in photography, art, and design. In her teaching, she integrates creative vision with structured project management, enabling her to mentor students effectively. This approach helps students develop critical thinking and robust art skills, preparing them for diverse creative challenges. Garima’s commitment to both academic excellence and hands-on learning fosters a dynamic and supportive environment for aspiring artists and practitioners.
entries.text: Garima Chaudhary Assistant Professor Department of Visual Arts Garima Chaudhary is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Visual Arts at the School of Design, MIT World Peace University (MIT-WPU). entries.title: Harshit Pratap Singh
entries.excerpt: Harshit is an educator and designer, specializing in areas of research, graphic design, and film-making. He holds a UGC-NET qualification, and has completed an M.A. in Education from Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Mumbai and a Bachelor of Design from Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay. Before joining the Department of Design at MIT World Peace University as an Assistant Professor, he has worked with multiple NGOs including Mantra4Change, Simple Education Foundation, Myna Mahila Foundation in varying capacities of researcher, designer, and educator.

entries.title: Hemlata Sandip Ohal
entries.excerpt: : AI in Health Care
entries.text: Hemlata Sandip Ohal Lecturer (Sr. Scale) Department of Polytechnic : AI in Health Care Mrs. Hemlata Sandip Ohal is an accomplished academician with over 26 years of teaching experience and is currently serving as an Assistant Professor at the School of Polytechnic, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She holds an M.E. in Information Technology from the University of Pune and is currently pursuing her Ph.D. at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune.

entries.title: Heramb Chintamani Phadake
entries.excerpt: Air conditioning, Energy optimization
entries.text: Heramb Chintamani Phadake Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Air conditioning, Energy optimization Prof. Heramb Phadake is a highly accomplished Assistant Professor with a diverse background in Mechanical Engineering. With a 20+ years of experience, he has spent 20 years working in the industry and 5 years teaching. Prof. Phadake holds a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering (currently pursuing), an M.Tech. in Energy Engineering - Design, and a B.E. in Mechanical Engineering. He specializes in Energy Engineering, Thermal Engineering, and Automotive Technology, and is a member of the Society of Automotive Engineering (SAE). Prof. Phadake's strengths lie in process planning and execution, and he has gained extensive coordination experience while working on infrastructure projects for over 20 years.

entries.title: Himangi Tanmay Neve
entries.excerpt: Polymers, Biomaterials, Biodegradation, Waste management and valorisation, Life Cycle Assessment
entries.text: Himangi Tanmay Neve Assistant Professor Department of Bioengineering Polymers, Biomaterials, Biodegradation, Waste management and valorisation, Life Cycle Assessment Himangi Neve is an Assistant Professor in the Bioengineering Department of Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. She has a master’s degree in polymer engineering and is now pursuing a PhD in chemical engineering. She has 11 years of combined job experience in industry and academia. Her research focuses on developing more effective and efficient bioconversion technologies for converting agricultural waste into high-value items, as well as evaluating the significance of life cycle assessment in processes ranging from waste collection to product distribution. She has produced over 5 research articles in international journals and 3 book chapters, and has garnered numerous accolades, including two Best Paper awards at international conferences and a runner-up team at the MITWPU Hackathon. She also won a Rs. 3 lakh research grant from MITWPU, as well as Rs. 1 lakh in the Hackathon. Himangi has also completed a number of waste management consulting projects for national and multinational companies. She has also published an Indian patent. In addition to her teaching and research responsibilities, Himangi is the department’s IQAC, Exam, Infrastructure, and Rural Immersion Coordinator.

entries.title: Jagruti Divakar Patil
entries.excerpt: Dr. Jagruti Patil is a distinguished Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She has earned a Ph.D. in Structural Engineering with a specialization in Earthquake Engineering from MIT-ATD University, Pune. Additionally, she holds an M.E. in Structural Engineering and a B.E. in Civil Engineering from Pune University. Apart from teaching responsibilities, Dr. Patil is also responsible for coordinating social media and admissions activities at MIT-WPU.

entries.title: Jyoti Anirudha Lele
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Music Signal Analysis and Synthesis, Speech Processing, Pattern Recognition, Computer Vision, Machine Learning
entries.text: Jyoti Anirudha Lele Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Music Signal Analysis and Synthesis, Speech Processing, Pattern Recognition, Computer Vision, Machine Learning Jyoti Lele is an Assistant Professor at the School of Electronics and Communication, with a B.E. degree in Electronics Engineering from Shivaji University in 2000, and an M.E. in Electronics (Digital Systems) from Pune University in 2009. Currently, she is pursuing her Ph.D. from the Department of Technology at SP Pune University, focusing on music signal analysis and synthesis. Her research interests span a variety of areas, including pattern recognition, machine learning, speech processing, computer vision, and artificial intelligence. With over 20 years of teaching experience, she has published more than 15 technical papers in peer-reviewed journals and conference proceedings.

entries.title: Kamakshi Goyal
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Ms. Kamakshi Goyal is an Assistant Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. She has previously worked at Chandmal Tarachand Bora College, Shirur, Pune, Maharashra and Nowrosjee Wadia College, Pune, Maharashtra as an Assistant Professor. Her research areas are Blockchain, AI and ML. With over five years of experience in teaching and research, she authored three books on Internet of Things, Business Analytics and Statistical Methods using R, Big Data Analytics.

entries.title: Mahesh Kulkarni
entries.excerpt: Assistant Professor and 3D Modeler/Animator Mahesh M. Kulkarni holds 20 plus years of Industry and Academic experience. Graduation and Post Graduation from Pune University and CDAC, Mahesh holds industry experience in Architectural and Environment design for games and animation. His broad experience includes Game Level Design, Game Assets creation, Digital Sculpting, 3D Modeling & Animation, optimization for games and Architectural/ Interior designing. He is enthusiastic in teaching and sharing industry standard design techniques and aesthetically stunning visual imagery. His wide range of portfolio includes Architectural Visualization for European Interior designing firms/ Architectural companies, Game asset visualization and production for domestic firms and Consultancy on Animation and game designing start-ups.

entries.title: Maisam Ali
entries.excerpt: Maisam Ali is an independent filmmaker with expertise in both short and feature films. He is a graduate of the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), majoring in Film Direction. His short films have been exhibited and honoured in France, the United States, the United Arab Emirates, and India. His feature film In Retreat was the first Indian film to be included in the ACID sidebar section at the Cannes Film Festival 2024, marking a 32-year milestone. The film was screened at the Busan International Film Festival, Sao Paulo Film Festival, Galway Film Festival, Moscow International Film Festival, and Mumbai Film Festival, among others. In addition, he has been teaching filmmaking at several institutions in India. He is presently overseeing Direction practices at Dadasaheb International Film School in the position of Assistant Professor.

entries.title: Mangesh Raghvendra Mahajan
entries.excerpt: Energy, HVAC
entries.text: Mangesh Raghvendra Mahajan Lecturer Department of Polytechnic Energy, HVAC Prof. Mangesh R. Mahajan is a hardcore, committed teacher and has teaching experience of 28 years. He has also undergone various training programmes, completed MOOC courses, and NITTTR's teacher training programmes. He has completed post graduation in Heat Power engineering from Pune University (Now SPPU). He is pursuing doctoral programme from Sandip University with research work in Diffuser Augmented Wind Turbines. Apart from this HVAC is also his key research interest area. He has published 05 papers till date mostly in SCOPUS journals. He has 03 books to his credit. He has a passion for teaching and developed extensive learning material for the benefit of students. He had been actively involved in the curriculum making process of different courses. He has also developed a lab manual for MSBTE students. He executed successfully a task of creating digital learning resources for MSBTE for a course Emerging trends in Mechanical Engineering. He had given expert lectures on AutoCAD to chemical engineering students. He has been instrumental in organizing various Faculty Development Programmes on campus. He is actively involved in maintaining rapport with industry for various co-curricular activities like Industry Visits, Expert Lectures, Industry oriented workshops, Internships activities. entries.title: Manisha Mohan Wani
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Electrical Power systems
entries.text: Manisha Mohan Wani Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Electrical Power systems Prof. Manisha Mohan Wani is an accomplished electrical engineer and educator with over 11 years of teaching experience. She graduated with a degree in Electrical Engineering in 2004 from Pune Vidyrthi Gruha’s College of Engineering, Pune University, and completed her ME in Electrical Power Systems in 2012 from the same institution. Since August 1, 2007, Prof. Wani has been working at MIT-WPU, where she has taken on a range of academic and administrative responsibilities. She is a permanent member of the Institution of Engineers, India and has served as a class teacher, ISO & NBA coordinator of CEES department, felicitation committee head of CEES department, and ARC officer for FE, direct second year, and ME admissions, among other duties. Additionally, Prof. Wani has been a paper setting committee member at SPPU and MIT-WPU and a university flying squad member at MIT-WPU. Her expertise in electrical engineering and her dedication to teaching have made her an asset to the academic community.

entries.title: Mr. Prathamesh Sonawani
entries.excerpt: Prathamesh Sonawani believes teaching is about awakening perception, not just delivering information. In his classes, he encourages students to listen, observe, and think critically about sound, performance, and storytelling as lived experiences, not just technical subjects. His motivation comes from seeing learners discover their own artistic voice and confidence. He blends practical industry insight with creative exploration, so students are prepared for both professional realities and meaningful expression. At MIT-WPU, he hopes to contribute to an environment where artistic skill, cultural awareness, and emotional intelligence grow together in thoughtful, socially aware creators.

entries.text: Nitinkumar Raghunath Anekar Assistant Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Stress Analysis, Failure Analysis, Corrosion and Wear Analysis, Mechanical Engineering Design, Mechanical Vibrations, Biomechanics, Energy Harvesting, Nano technology Nitinkumar Anekar is a highly skilled and experienced Mechanical Engineer with expertise in Manufacturing Techniques, Nano technology, Nano energy, Vibration Analysis, Mechanism and Synthesis, Design Engineering and Failure Analysis. Currently, he is an Assistant Professor at the School of Mechanical Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. He has conducted extensive research in the field of Mechanical Engineering and has published several research papers in renowned international journals. He is also an accomplished lecturer and has delivered talks on various subjects related to Mechanical Engineering. In addition to his academic pursuits, Nitinkumar maintains an online presence through his website nitinanekar.wixsite.com/nitinkumar-anekar, which showcases his research and academic achievements.

entries.title: NSRTC 2025
entries.excerpt: A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for Viksit Bharat.
entries.text: NSRTC 2025 NSRTC 2025 A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for Viksit Bharat. NSRTC 2025 Science, innovation, and the vision for Viksit Bharat Date 18 July 2025 · three-day conference Location MIT World Peace University, Pune Format Conference Status Past event Official MIT-WPU event report ↗ Overview A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for Viksit Bharat. The programme opened with plenary sessions on Nobel Prize-recognised work in medicine, chemistry, physics, and engineering, followed by specialist talks and a formal inaugural session. Technical sessions and round tables connected fundamental research, applied science, technology development, and questions of national capacity. Themes Artificial intelligence Advanced materials and processing Digital transformation, energy, and sustainability Healthcare, pharmaceutical science, and biotechnology Programme Special talks Ashutosh Sharma spoke on science and technology for Viksit Bharat; Arindam Ghosh on materials for quantum technologies; and Ajit Kembhavi on the life and scientific journey of Jayant Vishnu Narlikar. Scientific programme Parallel sessions covered artificial intelligence, materials and devices, energy and sustainability, healthcare, pharmaceuticals, and biotechnology, with posters and round-table discussions.

entries.title: NSRTC 2026
entries.excerpt: Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology.
entries.text: NSRTC 2026 NSRTC 2026 Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology. NSRTC 2026 A three-day conclave advancing science, technology, and innovation Date 17–19 July 2026 Location MIT World Peace University, Pune Format Conference Status Past event Official MIT-WPU event report ↗ Overview Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology. The programme combined invited talks and parallel technical sessions with round-table discussions, poster presentations, project exhibitions, and a Scholars-to-Scientists Connect initiative. The conference brought together contributors from universities, national laboratories, research institutes, industry, and public organisations. Themes Reimagining the future with artificial intelligence Materials, energy, and sustainability Emergent device technologies New biology and health sciences Programme Day 1 Opening sessions considered major scientific and engineering prizes, followed by the inaugural programme, research and entrepreneurship awards, keynote addresses, and a lecture on national wealth through science and technology. Days 2–3 Four parallel research tracks covered artificial intelligence, sustainable energy, advanced materials, semiconductors, health technologies, biotechnology, quantum technologies, and indigenous innovation.

entries.text: Pallavi Rajwade Assistant Professor, Program Co-ordinator BFA & MFA (Applied Arts) Department of Visual Arts Brand Perception and Packaging Visuals Ms. Pallavi Rajwade is passionate about communication design and believes that curiosity and creativity make life fun and enriched. She has received her formal education in Applied Arts along with a Post Graduate Diploma in Public Relations & Advertising. She has worked as a Visualiser and Graphic Designer for over 15 years. in India as well as abroad. She has almost 5+ years of enriching experience in Academics as BFA and MFA Program Coordinator for AY 2023-2024. Also, she is currently pursuing PhD from Symbiosis International University. Currently, she is teaching courses such as Advertising, Branding, and Visualisation and has mentored final-year students for Visual Communication Design projects for the last 2 years at MIT WPU Department of Visual Arts, School of Design.

entries.title: Parul Ghole
entries.excerpt: Parul Ghole is a distinguished Visual Artist with extensive experience in academia and design training. She has served as an Assistant Professor at JNAFAU(4 years), Hyderabad, and Bharati Vidyapeeth, Pune(1 year), and is currently (since jan 2023 )a faculty member at MIT-WPU,Pune, where her research interests include Illustration, Visualization, and Fundamentals of Design. Additionally, since 2019, she has contributed as a Senior Design Trainer at MAD School in Hyderabad. Her diverse roles highlight her commitment to fostering artistic talent and design principles across various educational institutions.

entries.title: Piyush Shaha
entries.excerpt: Mr. Piyush Shaha is an accomplished academician with extensive experience in the fields of Statistics, Operations Management, and Business Administration. He holds multiple postgraduate degrees, including an M.Sc. in Statistics, M.B.A. in Operations, and M.Com. in Business Administration, along with a B.Sc. in Statistics. He has also qualified the SET (State Eligibility Test) in Mathematical Sciences and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Statistics.

entries.title: Prajakta Balchandra Khanwalkar
entries.excerpt: Industrial Design, Visual Ergonomics, Design Eductaion, Aesthetics Studies
entries.text: Prajakta Balchandra Khanwalkar Assistant Professor Department of Design Industrial Design, Visual Ergonomics, Design Eductaion, Aesthetics Studies Prajakta Khanwalkar specializes in Architecture and Industrial Design. She holds a Bachelor Degree from PVP College of Architecture, Pune and Master's degree from Industrial Design Center, IIT Bombay. She has an experience of around two decades in the field of design and design education. Her professional work includes industrial design projects in consumer products, medical devices, commercial interiors, brand staretegies etc. She has been teaching in the realm of architecture and design. She has handled foundation classes, form studies, human centered design, new product development, inclusive product design and design projects. She is a core comittee member of the Association of Designers of India and is an active member in Pune Design Festival and Design Eductaion Festival happening in Pune. She is also a research scholar at IIT Guwahati.

entries.title: Pranati Prabhakar Waghodekar
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: A Red Hat–certified and Cloud-certified Architect (AWS & Azure), I bring over 7.5 years of academic experience and 2 years of industry exposure to deliver outcome-driven, industry-aligned education. My teaching philosophy emphasizes experiential learning, critical thinking, and hands-on skill development to prepare students for real-world challenges. I actively integrate emerging technologies such as Cloud, DevOps, and Blockchain into the curriculum, fostering innovation and research orientation among students. Currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Blockchain, I remain committed to advancing MIT-WPU’s vision of value-based, technology-driven education and holistic student development.

entries.text: Pravin Raghunath Minde Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Construction Management, Ferrocement , Sustainable construction Dr. Pravin Minde is working as an Assistant Professor and PG Coordinator (CEM) in the Department of Civil Engineering at MIT-WPU, Pune. He has over 12 years academic & administrative experience. He completed his Ph.D. in the domain of Construction Project Management from MIT-WPU in April 2025. He earned his M.E. in Construction Management in 2014 and B.E. in Civil Engineering in 2012, both from Pune University.

entries.title: Prof. Akshay Motilal Baheti
entries.excerpt: Dr. Akshay M. Baheti is a Professor of Pharmacognosy at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 25 years of experience in teaching, research, and administration in pharmaceutical education. He holds a B.Pharm from North Maharashtra University, M.Pharm in Pharmacognosy and Phytochemistry from Savitribai Phule Pune University, and a Ph.D. in Pharmaceutical Sciences from JNTU, Hyderabad, and is a Registered Patent Agent (IN/PA 5393). Dr. Baheti has served in key leadership roles as Head of the School of Pharmacy and Head, Department of Pharmacognosy. He has published over 30 peer-reviewed research papers, contributed to multiple book chapters, and has 14 patents filed/granted to his credit. He has guided several M.Pharm dissertations, successfully supervised one Ph.D. scholar to completion, and is currently supervising seven Ph.D. scholars in the areas of Pharmaceutical Sciences. Dr. Baheti has been recognized with several awards, including the MIT Foundation Day Award (2014) and the Best Innovative Teacher Award by Savitribai Phule Pune University (2017). He has also received a fellowship in Traditional Medicines and Integrative Health. Educational Qualifications M. Pharm, PhD

entries.title: Prof. Alamgir Sani
entries.excerpt: Dr. Alamgir Sani is actively pursuing and publishing research in the domain of Consumer Buying Behaviour and Brand Preference drivers; Multi-Sided Technology Platform Business Models; Passenger Cars Buying Behaviour and Preference Drivers, Impact of emerging technologies, including Blockchain Technologies on business models and Supply Chains, Application of AI and Human Mimicking Technology (HMT) in Customer Engagement etc.
entries.text: Prof. Alamgir Sani Associate Professor Department of Business Dr. Alamgir Sani is actively pursuing and publishing research in the domain of Consumer Buying Behaviour and Brand Preference drivers; Multi-Sided Technology Platform Business Models; Passenger Cars Buying Behaviour and Preference Drivers, Impact of emerging technologies, including Blockchain Technologies on business models and Supply Chains, Application of AI and Human Mimicking Technology (HMT) in Customer Engagement etc. Dr. Alamgir Sani is a distinguished academician and professional with over 24 years of combined experience across academia and industry, marked by a rich blend of teaching, research, and corporate exposure. He holds a Ph.D. in Management (Marketing) from Patna University and an MBA in Marketing and Finance from Aligarh Muslim University, and has qualified the UGC- NET. Dr. Sani’s corporate tenure includes impactful roles such as Cargo Manager with the Airports Authority of India and customer operations with EXL Services and DHL, which provided him deep expertise in logistics, air cargo operations, and global supply chains.

entries.title: Prof. Ameya Anil Patil
entries.excerpt: Finance, Management, International Business
entries.text: Prof. Ameya Anil Patil Associate Professor Department of Business Finance, Management, International Business Dr. Ameya Patil has more than 12 years of Teaching experience and 3.5 years of Industrial experience. He teaches subjects related to Finance and International Business. He has 25 Scopus indexed research articles to his credit and has authored more than 25 books. He has been invited as a keynote speaker at several national conferences. He is a member of the Indian Institute of Banking and Finance (IIBF) and ASCB(UK). 18 of his research works have appeared in Scopus and ABDC journals, and with reputed publishers such as Wiley, Inderscience, Taylor & Francis, IGI Global, Emerald and Springer.

entries.title: Prof. Anil Satishrao Hiwale
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Communication, Healthcare, Signal Processing
entries.text: Prof. Anil Satishrao Hiwale Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Communication, Healthcare, Signal Processing Dr. Anil S. Hiwale is a renowned professor with a Ph.D. in Electronics Engineering, specialized in Wireless Communication, from SGB Amravati University. With over 33 years of teaching experience, he is an expert in Wireless Communication, Signal Processing, Healthcare, and AI/ML. Dr. Hiwale has published numerous papers in reputed journals and conferences, and he has successfully supervised three Ph.D. scholars, with five more under his guidance currently. He is currently serving as the Associate Dean (Students Development) at the Faculty of Engineering & Technology, MITWPU, and has previously held positions such as Professor, Dean R & D, and Principal of MIT College of Engineering. Dr. Hiwale is a Fellow of IETE (The Institution of Electronics & Telecommunication Engineers) and IE (Institution of Engineers, India), and he is also the Chairman of The IET Pune Local Network.

entries.title: Prof. Anil Tukaram Pawar
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anil Pawar is an Associate Professor at the School of Health Sciences and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 17 years of experience in academic teaching, research, and industry. He holds a B. Pharm from Savitribai Phule Pune University, an M. Pharm from Rajiv Gandhi University of Health Sciences, Karnataka, and a Ph.D. in Pharmacology. He has published more than 40 research articles and several review papers, authored books and book chapters, and holds five patents. He has guided M. Pharm students and is currently supervising Ph.D. scholars. Dr. Pawar has also received consultancy grants, reflecting his engagement in applied research and industry collaboration, and has established strong national and international research collaborations. He is a member of several prestigious professional associations, including the Association of Pharmaceutical Teachers of India (APTI), Indian Pharmaceutical Association (IPA), Indian Pharmacological Society (IPS), Society of Pharmaceutical Education and Research (SPER), International Association for the Study of Traditional Asian Medicine (IASTAM-India), and the Laboratory Animal Science Association of India (LASAI). He has received multiple awards for excellence in teaching and research, including the Ideal Teacher Award by Maharashtra Academy of Engineering and Educational Research (MAEER), Pune.

entries.title: Prof. Anjali Ajit Sane
entries.excerpt: Financial Literacy, Banking
entries.text: Prof. Anjali Ajit Sane Professor & Dean Department of Commerce & Accounting Financial Literacy, Banking An accomplished teacher with over 20 years of experience in the areas of Economics and Banking, Dr. Anjali Sane is also a capable administrator. In addition to academic administration, her research areas include Managerial Economics, Micro and Macro Economics and various areas in international business. A dedicated and sincere teacher, she is known for use of practical examples and case studies to supplement classroom learning.

entries.title: Prof. Anjali Milind Purohit
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Power System Protection, Artificial Intelligence, Power Electronics
entries.text: Prof. Anjali Milind Purohit Associate Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Power System Protection, Artificial Intelligence, Power Electronics Prof. Anjali Purohit is an accomplished academic and researcher in the field of Electrical Engineering. She completed her graduation in Electrical Engineering from Pune University in 1992 and later received her M.E. (Power Systems) from the same university in 2000. Currently, she is pursuing her PhD in power system protection from Amaravati University. Anjali has over 20 years of teaching experience and two years of industry experience. She is an Associate Professor in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering at MIT-WPU. Prof. Purohit is a member of IE (I) and Life Member of ISTE. She has organized numerous STTP and conferences for the benefit of faculty and students. Prof. Purohit has also undertaken significant administrative responsibilities throughout her career. Her research interests include power system protection, power electronics, and artificial intelligence, among others.

entries.title: Prof. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya
entries.excerpt: Dr. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya is a distinguished Political Scientist from Banaras Hindu University (BHU), where she served over four decades in pivotal roles including Senior Professor and Chair of Political Science, Dean of Social Sciences, Director of the Centre for Nepal Studies, Director of the Centre for Integrated Rural Development, and Adjunct Professor at the Malaviya Centre for Peace Research. Her academic journey includes a PhD from BHU (1981), MA from Allahabad University (1974), post-doctoral research at the London School of Economics, Woodrow Wilson International Centre for Scholars, Brown University, and an Advanced International Diploma in Conflict Resolution from Uppsala University. She has held prestigious positions such as Research Director at the UN University Institute on Conflict Resolution and Ethnicity (UK), Scholar-in-Residence at Woodrow Wilson Centre (USA), foundational ICCR Chair at Tribhuvan University (Nepal), India Chair by ICSSR New Delhi, and visiting professorships at institutions like Wellesley College, Concordia University, Stanford University, PRIO Oslo, and universities across Sweden, Germany, Australia, Canada, and Poland.
entries.text: Prof. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya Distinguish Professor & Senior Advisor Department of Liberal Arts Dr. Anjoo Sharan Upadhyaya is a distinguished Political Scientist from Banaras Hindu University (BHU), where she served over four decades in pivotal roles including Senior Professor and Chair of Political Science, Dean of Social Sciences, Director of the Centre for Nepal Studies, Director of the Centre for Integrated Rural Development, and Adjunct Professor at the Malaviya Centre for Peace Research. Her academic journey includes a PhD from BHU (1981), MA from Allahabad University (1974), post-doctoral research at the London School of Economics, Woodrow Wilson International Centre for Scholars, Brown University, and an Advanced International Diploma in Conflict Resolution from Uppsala University. She has held prestigious positions such as Research Director at the UN University Institute on Conflict Resolution and Ethnicity (UK), Scholar-in-Residence at Woodrow Wilson Centre (USA), foundational ICCR Chair at Tribhuvan University (Nepal), India Chair by ICSSR New Delhi, and visiting professorships at institutions like Wellesley College, Concordia University, Stanford University, PRIO Oslo, and universities across Sweden, Germany, Australia, Canada, and Poland.

She is presently working as Associate Professor and Associate Head at the School of Electronics and Communication Engineering, Dr. Vishwanath Karad Maharashtra Institute of Technology World Peace University. She has authored two books, granted one patent, published and presented several papers in indexed Journals and conferences, and has contributed to the initiation, development, and up-gradation of elective subject Laboratories in VLSI Design, Embedded Systems, Image processing, and Machine Learning. entries.excerpt: Dr. Anuradha Kanade is the Program Director of the Department of Computer Science and Applications at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She holds a Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University, an M.Phil., MCA, MBA (Business Analytics), and a B.Sc. (Physics). With over 24 years of academic and industry experience, she has made significant contributions to teaching, research, academic leadership, and curriculum development. She is starving for the excellence to ensure the overall growth of the students, department and faculty members. Her teaching philosophy emphasizes learner-centric, value-based, and outcome-oriented education that nurtures technical competence, ethical values, innovation, and lifelong learning. She is passionate about motivating students through research, industry interaction, internships, and project-based learning Through her contributions in research, patents, publications, academic leadership, and industry collaborations, she actively supports MIT-WPU’s vision of promoting excellence in education, innovation, research, and holistic development of socially responsible global professionals. She is an approved Ph.D. supervisor and has guided research scholars in emerging areas of computer science. Dr. Kanade has authored 50+ research publications, several books, and holds multiple Indian and UK patents, with a number of them granted. She has successfully completed funded research projects and actively collaborates with academia and industry to promote innovation and applied research.

entries.excerpt: AIoT, TinyML, LPWANs, Si-Photonics, Optical Networks, Switching & Routing Technologies
entries.text: Prof. Bharat Sambhu Chaudhari Senior Professor (HAG) and Dean - Research and Development Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering AIoT, TinyML, LPWANs, Si-Photonics, Optical Networks, Switching & Routing Technologies Dr. Bharat S. Chaudhari has pursued the BE in Industrial Electronics from Amravati University in 1989 and then METelE and PhD in Engineering from Jadavpur University, Kolkata, India, in 1993 and 2000, respectively. After being a Full Professor in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering with the Pune Institute of Computer Technology and the Dean and Principal of the International Institute of Information Technology, Pune, he joined MIT World Peace University (then MIT Pune) in 2014 as a Professor. Currently, he is working as a Senior Professor (Higher Administrative Group) in Electronics and Communication Engineering and Dean – Research and Development. He held additional responsibilities as Dean of Academics (July 2021 - Sept 2022), Associate Dean - Faculty of Engineering and Technology (Feb 2020 - June 2023), and Head of the School of Electrical/Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering (July 2017 - December 2023) at MIT World Peace University.

He holds a Bachelor's, Master's, and PhD in Chemical Engineering, and has amassed 17 years of teaching and research experience. entries.title: Prof. Dnyaneshwar Ramchandra Waghole
entries.excerpt: heat transfer equipments, nanofluidics/nanomaterial, EV (BTMS/Fuel cell),Healthcare ,energy ,Operations research
entries.text: Prof. Dnyaneshwar Ramchandra Waghole Associate Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering heat transfer equipments, nanofluidics/nanomaterial, EV (BTMS/Fuel cell),Healthcare ,energy ,Operations research Dr. Dnyaneshwar R Waghole holds a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering, along with an M.E. in Mechanical Engineering with a specialization in Heat Power and a B.E. in Mechanical Engineering. He has a total of 21.5 years of experience, including 20 years in teaching and 1.5 years in the industry. His research areas include NanoFluids, Nanomaterials & Allied Nano Materials, Biomedical Healthcare Technology, Heat Transfer Equipment, and Energy Harvesting Technology. Dr. Waghole has published 10 research papers in national and international journals and has filed a patent for "Heat Transfer Enhancement using Flow divider type Inserts." He is a member of the Solar Energy Society of India (SESI) and the Society of Automotive Engineering (SAE).

Borikar is an expert in the field of manufacturing engineering and leads the Central Workshop at MIT-WPU. His role involves upgrading and setting up advanced machines and equipment in line with industry practices to train students in modern technology. Dr. Borikar's expertise lies in the domains of additive manufacturing and micro manufacturing. He supports and contributes to innovation and projects related to manufacturing engineering and smart work. With his extensive knowledge and experience, Dr. Borikar plays a vital role in shaping the future of manufacturing technology at MIT-WPU.

entries.title: Prof. Ganesh Shivaji Ingle
entries.excerpt: Prf. Dr. Ganesh Ingle is an Associate Professor in Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He has completed his Ph.D. in Transportation Engineering from College of Engineering Pune (COEP) in the year 2018, received his B. E. in Civil Engineering from Govt. College of Engineering, Amravati in the year 2000 and Masters (M. E. Civil) in technical Engineering in the year of 2003, from College of Engineering Pune (COEP). He is working with MIT-WPU since August 2008 and has been teaching various subjects like soil Mechanics, Foundation Engineering, soil structure and interaction, concrete technology, construction materials and advanced transportation Engineering. As a part of Ph. D work, he has indigenously designed and developed a Laboratory Full scale Accelerated Pavement Testing Set Up (APT) in COEP campus through an R&D Grant of 13.5 Lac. This is a unique facility which is not available in India where the performance of any pavement can be tested within a short period. It was also applied for Indian Patent. He has published more than 15 research papers in Scopus and SCI Journals and also published three Indian Patent and one Australian Patent granted on stabilization of soil. He is also doing Testing and consultancy in the field of Geotechnical Engineering and completed more than 4.0 Lac testing and consultancy related to slope stability analysis in Chiplun, Dapoli area as well as geotechnical investigation for SBC of soil for some of the project. He also conducted the training sessions for Border Road Organization (BRO) Engineers related to Geotechnical aspects during road constructions. He also received the BCUD Grants of 1.40 Lac.

entries.title: Prof. Gauri Bilaye
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: My philosophy as a teacher will be based on stimulating curiosity, critical thinking, and problem solving in the real world. My goal is to build a non-discriminatory, interactive learning classroom, in which students form an active group, challenge their thoughts and possibilities, and relate theory with practice. As a teacher, my driving power is to guide students towards achieving not only academic results by also teaching them how to achieve ethical values, creativity and life-long learning. In line with the vision of the holistic and value-based education at MIT-WPU, I would be able to make a contribution through creative teaching methods, interdisciplinary cooperation, and the learning process based on research that would help students become responsible professional beings and influential global citizens.
