entries.title: Prof. Gautam Surendra Bapat
entries.excerpt: Dr. Gautam Bapat is Associate Dean – Faculty Affairs and Associate Professor of Marketing at the School of Business, MIT World Peace University, Pune. With over a decade of experience, he has held key academic leadership roles, contributing to program design, faculty development, and institutional advancement. His academic philosophy integrates industry relevance with scholarly rigor. Dr. Bapat holds a Ph.D. in Management, focusing on digital marketing for student recruitment, along with qualifications in marketing, computer applications, and zoology. His interdisciplinary approach informs his teaching in branding, digital transformation, and marketing strategy. A prolific researcher, he has published over 38 papers, presented at international forums, authored textbooks, and holds five patents in areas like IoT and social service systems. His work explores AI in marketing, higher education strategies, and consumer behavior. Recognized with awards from IIM Ahmedabad and IRSSM, he is a sought-after speaker and an advocate for innovation in management education.

entries.title: Prof. Girish Shamraoji Mude
entries.excerpt: Consumer Behavior, Customer Centricity and Agritourism
entries.text: Prof. Girish Shamraoji Mude Associate Professor and Program Director Department of Business Consumer Behavior, Customer Centricity and Agritourism Dr. Girish Mude is Associate Professor and Program Director of BBA (Digital Marketing) and MBA (Marketing) at School of Business, MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. Skilled in Sales & Marketing, Training, Consultancy, Market Research. Has 18 years of work experience in Teaching and Industry. He holds a Ph.D. in Management, an MBA, and a PGDFT from the University of Pune, and is UGC-NET (twice) and SET qualified. He has completed a PGP in Data Science from KPMG and a Design Thinking course from IIT Madras. His research has been presented at esteemed institutions like IIMs and IITs. Dr. Mude has facilitated the International Winter School Program at the University of Hull, UK, and has visited London, Italy, Switzerland, France, and the UK for academic assignments and institutional collaborations. Recipient “Research Scholarship” from University of Vermont, USA; “Research Fellowship from EURAC, Italy” and “Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam MIT- WPU Research and Innovation Promising Prize 2019”. Dr Girish is also an experienced Consultant with a demonstrated history of working in the marketing and advertising industry.

entries.title: Prof. Girish Sharad Barpande
entries.excerpt: Dr. Girish Sharad Barpande is a multi-faceted professional with diverse interests ranging from aerospace and design to social issues and films. With a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering (Aerospace-Scramjet) from Savitribai Phule Pune University, he has 27 years of professional experience, out of which 24 years are in teaching. His areas of interest and expertise include engineering design, design thinking, computational fluid dynamics, tribology, and total quality management. He has authored 11 research papers in various international journals and conferences. He founded and headed the SUPRA SAEINDIA team at MIT since 2011 with an impressive track record at national and international levels.

Being an avid reader and a contributor to the field of research, He has also written 32 publications in Journals and attended 35 conferences. Dr. Harshal has been appointed a distinguished review member. Many researchers have also acknowledged Dr. Harshal's work on agricultural subsidies to date; his Google citation index is 165 & h-index is 7. He has filed four patents. With more than 17th experience in education and academia, his expertise in Commodity Market, Indirect Tax Financial Management, Income Tax, International Financial Management, Financial derivatives, Bank Management, Investment Management, Family Business Management, and Corporate Social Responsibility.

entries.title: Prof. Hossein Martin Fazeli
entries.excerpt: Hossein Martin Fazeli is a distinguished Iranian writer, producer, and director who has been an A-list filmmaker in North America for many years and currently serves as a Professor of Practice at Dadasaheb Phalke International Film School. With over two decades of experience in producing, directing, and writing fiction and non-fiction television and films, Fazeli has built an illustrious career marked by his extensive international collaborations across more than a dozen countries. His works have been broadcast globally on leading networks such as CBC, BBC, ARTE, and Canal+, earning 37 international awards and critical acclaim.

entries.excerpt: Design, Composite Material, Robotics, Mechatronics
entries.text: Prof. Mahesh Nana Pradhan Associate Professor and Program Coordinator (R&A) Department of Mechanical Engineering Design, Composite Material, Robotics, Mechatronics With over 25 years of teaching experience in the prestigious MIT-WPU, Prof. Mahesh Pradhan is a renowned figure in the field of mechanical engineering. His two-year industrial experience at Bajaj Tempo Ltd has contributed to his vast knowledge in the industry. Prof. Pradhan completed his M.E in Mechanical from COEP, Pune and has authored several research papers in the field of design and composite materials. As the Dept. Coordinator of the Centre for Industry Academia Partnership (CIAP) and Internship Coordinator, he has played a key role in shaping the academic curriculum of MIT-WPU. He is also the coordinator of the Rickshaw and Taxi Fare Meter Testing Centre and has served as a committee member for the implementation of electronic Rickshaw and Taxi fare meters in Maharashtra State. Prof. Pradhan is also credited with setting up the Mechatronics and Robotics laboratory in MIT-WPU and is currently serving as the laboratory incharge.

He holds a B.E in Chemical Engineering and a Ph.D in Polymer Engineering & Technology. He is currently involved in teaching both Undergraduate and Postgraduate courses in Chemical and Polymer Engineering. Dr. Kulkarni's research is focused on the development of Sustainable Eco-friendly materials in the field of Chemical and Polymer Engineering. He is a Life Member of AICER (Associate, International Congress of Environmental Research), Indian Plastics Institute (PUN-231-LIF), and Asian Polymer Association (L-604).

entries.title: Prof. Manasi Mishra
entries.excerpt: Plant defence proteins and peptides, bioactive molecules, protein engineering, botanical therapeutics, molecular diversity, and evolution.
entries.text: Prof. Manasi Mishra Associate Professor Department of Biosciences and Technology Plant defence proteins and peptides, bioactive molecules, protein engineering, botanical therapeutics, molecular diversity, and evolution. Dr. Manasi Mishra is working as Associate Professor in Department of Biosciences & Technology, MIT-World Peace University. She has been actively working in the area of plant immunity proteins and peptides involved in defense against insect pest infestation and those with relevance in the context of crop improvement. Her research has been focused on structure-function correlation of diverse plant defense proteins, molecular diversity and their functional significance. She is also working extensively towards understanding the defense peptides distribution within individual plants and their tissues, factors influencing their expression, gene architecture, post-translational mechanisms, bioactivities and designing synthetic peptides directed towards therapeutic molecular targets.

entries.title: Prof. Mangesh Vilas Bedekar
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Dr. Mangesh V. Bedekar earned his Ph.D in Computer Science and Engineering from BITS Pilani, India, after completing his graduation from SSGMCE, Shegaon, and obtaining his Masters from BITS Pilani. He currently serves as the Professor & Dean, School of Computer Science & Engineering at MIT-WPU. Dr. Bedekar's research interests are focused on areas such as Web Data Mining, Web Personalization, User Interface Design, User Interface Improvements, Browser Customization, Affective Computing, and Information Visualization. Throughout his career, he has supervised numerous bachelors and masters students and has many national and international publications to his credit. In addition to his academic pursuits, Dr. Bedekar is an outdoors enthusiast and a certified First-Aider who enjoys hiking and trekking regularly. entries.title: Prof. Manish Kumar Sharma
entries.excerpt: Prof. Manish has around 5.5 years of working experience in Industry and academia. His PhD is from the Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur (QS World-Ranked 215, 2026). He has earlier worked with the Centre for Digital Transformation at the Indian Institute of Management, Ahmedabad (QS World-ranked 27th in Management, 2026), where he contributed to a research project on Responsible AI and FinTech analysis. He has qualified UGC-NET in Management. He has done his post-graduation with MBA in Finance (University Gold Medallist) and B. Tech. in Electronics and Communication Engineering. His research interests lie in the areas of International Business and Economics, International Trade, Digital Economy, FinTech, Digital Transformation, Artificial Intelligence, Econometrics, Data Science, and Machine Learning. He has gained industry exposure for around four years, working both in India and internationally in East Africa. He earlier worked with the Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance, Govt. of India, on a research project titled “Digital Economy and Fintech in India: Opportunities and Challenges” as a PhD research intern. He is a published author with three published articles in reputed journals and has many more in the pipeline. He is also a peer reviewer at Electronic Commerce Research, Springer Nature and International Journal of Finance and Economics (IJFE), Wiley Publications. He has travelled to more than ten countries around the world during his research work, including the Netherlands, Spain, Belgium, France, Germany, the UK, Ethiopia, Morocco, Turkey, the UAE, and Qatar and has presented his research papers at many international conferences in India, Spain, the UK, and Morocco. He received two best research paper awards, one at IBRS 2024, Morocco and the other one at BIT MESRA in India. He aims to make an impactful contribution to the world through his quality research work. He is efficient working with diverse Computing and Statistical tools like Stata, Python, Tableau, Power BI, Jupyter Labs, Google Colab, SPSS, SAP ERP, Oracle Citrix ERP, and Tally ERP. He is a member of the International Trade and Finance Association, the Spanish Economic Association, the Econometric Society - Yale University, USA, and the Indian Society of Labour Economics.

Kulkarni is presently the In-Charge Principal of MIT-Pune, former Dean of Students Affairs at MIT-WPU, and a Professor at the School of Engineering and Technology at MIT-WPU and has headed the Department of Civil Engineering since its inception. entries.title: Prof. Narendra Laxman Mathakari
entries.excerpt: Radiation processing of polymers and composites; viscoelastic and acoustic properties of liquid mixtures.
entries.text: Prof. Narendra Laxman Mathakari Professor of Practice Department of Physics Radiation processing of polymers and composites; viscoelastic and acoustic properties of liquid mixtures. Dr. Narendra Mathakari obtained his M.Sc. (Physics) degree from Department of Physics, SPPU in 1989 and Ph.D degree from the same department and university in 2009, He has a vast teaching experience of over 30 years and research experience of over 15 years. He has published 11 research papers in international journals. and 4 papers in international conference proceedings. He has also presented papers at 9 national and international conferences. He has also published 10 science popularization articles in newspapers and delivered science popularization talks at various places. His research interests include radiation processing of polymers and composites and viscoelastic and acoustic properties of binary and ternary liquids. He currently has research facilities, including an Abbe refractometer, a density measurement device, a viscometry system, and an ultrasonic interferometer all with constant temperature baths. He has also published a book on “Experiments in Engineering Physics”. Currently his book on “Concepts of Engineering Physics” is in the process of publication. During his career in MIT WPU, he received the MIT Foundation Day Award in 2008 and MIT Ideal Teacher Award in 2009.

entries.excerpt: Image Processing, Power Systems, IoT, Robotics
entries.text: Prof. Netra Manoj Lokhande Associate Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Image Processing, Power Systems, IoT, Robotics Dr. Netra Lokhande is an Associate Professor in the School of School of Engineering and Technology at MIT World Peace University, Pune. She holds a PhD in Electronics, a Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, and a Bachelor's degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering. With 25 years of experience in teaching, Dr. Lokhande has expertise in Image Processing, Power Systems, Robotics, and Power Electronics. She has published 30 research papers and is a member of various professional bodies such as ISTE, IEI, IAENG, and IAEME.

entries.title: Prof. Nivedita Gunesh Gogate
entries.excerpt: Dr. Nivedita Gogate is currently working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering, Dr Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune from 2018. She joined the MIT-WPU in 1996 and since then she has been associated with teaching, administrative, research and examination work of the university. She completed her BE (Civil Engineering) from COEP followed by M.Env.Eng.Sc. (MS in Environmental Engineering) from University of New South Wales (UNSW), Sydney, Australia. She developed training manuals for Environmental Awareness in Industry for managers and workers in association with an Australian consulting firm. She has an industrial experience of 2 years and teaching experience of 26 years. She completed her PhD in Civil-Environmental Engineering from COEP. She has published 5 research papers in international journals of good repute and presented papers in international conferences. She, along with her colleagues has published an Indian patent. Presently, she is working as a PhD Coordinator of Department of Civil Engineering.
entries.text: Prof. Nivedita Gunesh Gogate Associate Professor Department of Civil Engineering Dr. Nivedita Gogate is currently working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering, Dr Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune from 2018. She joined the MIT-WPU in 1996 and since then she has been associated with teaching, administrative, research and examination work of the university. She completed her BE (Civil Engineering) from COEP followed by M.Env.Eng.Sc. (MS in Environmental Engineering) from University of New South Wales (UNSW), Sydney, Australia. She developed training manuals for Environmental Awareness in Industry for managers and workers in association with an Australian consulting firm. She has an industrial experience of 2 years and teaching experience of 26 years. She completed her PhD in Civil-Environmental Engineering from COEP. She has published 5 research papers in international journals of good repute and presented papers in international conferences. She, along with her colleagues has published an Indian patent. Presently, she is working as a PhD Coordinator of Department of Civil Engineering.

entries.title: Prof. Pankaj Nivrutti Dhatrak

entries.text: Prof. Pankaj Nivrutti Dhatrak Associate Professor Department of Mechanical Engineering Bio-mechanical Engineering, Medical Devices, Vibration and Fatigue, FEA Dr. Pankaj Dhatrak is an accomplished Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering at MIT-World Peace University, Pune, India. With a PhD in Biomechanical Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University, he has a strong background in Finite Element Analysis of Automotive components, Biomechanics, Biomaterials, and their applications. He has over 5 years of industrial experience in vibration and fatigue analysis of various automotive components using different CAE tools, along with more than 15 years of academic experience teaching UG/PG students. Dr. Dhatrak is an author of over 45+ peer-reviewed research articles and book chapters in reputed Journal and Conference Proceedings. Recently, he has been actively involved in designing and developing biomedical devices. He has served as a Technical Advisory Committee Member for 5+ International conferences and has reviewed manuscripts for over 5+ reputable peer-reviewed journals. Dr. Dhatrak is a Life member of Indian Society of Technical Education (ISTE) and Society of Automotive Engineering (SAE-India Western Section), and his research interests span across Biomedical research in Dentistry, integration of Mechanical Engineering for varied Biomechanical applic

entries.title: Prof. Pradeep Kumar Tiwari
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Dr. Pradeep Kumar Tiwari is an Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University. He has previously worked at Birla Global University and Manipal University Jaipur as an Associate Professor and Assistant Professor. Dr. Tiwari holds a Ph.D. in the Faculty of Engineering, along with M.Tech in CSE and M.Phil in Computer Science. Currently he is pursuing a Postdoctoral. His research areas cloud computing, distributed systems, AI, and IoT. With over fifteen years of experience in teaching and research, Dr. Tiwari has authored more than fifty publications in Web of Science and Scopus indexed journals, conferences, and book chapters in the field of computer science. Additionally, he has received three patent grants and published three patents. One scholar has completed and five scholars are currently pursuing their Ph.D. under the supervision of Dr. Tiwari.

entries.title: Prof. Prakash Mohan Mainkar
entries.department: Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prakash Mohan Mainkar obtained his Bachelor's degree in Electronics Engineering from Shivaji University in 2001, followed by a Master's degree in Electronics (VLSI Design) in 2007. He later completed his Doctor of Philosophy (Engineering) from RTM Nagpur University, Nagpur, India, in 2019. Currently, he serves as the Director of Admissions and an Associate Professor in Electronics & Communication Engineering at MIT-WPU. Along with his teaching responsibilities, he actively participates in various institute committees and contributes to the development of laboratory infrastructure and curriculum. Dr. Mainkar regularly engages with industry professionals to explore employment opportunities for students and facilitate international university collaborations for MIT-WPU. He has undertaken several international study tours to universities in China, Singapore, Hong Kong, and Nepal, in his capacity as an International Study Tour Coordinator. Over the past 8 years, he has also served as a National Study Tour Coordinator and organized visits to numerous industries throughout India to provide students with exposure to real-world applications of their coursework.

entries.title: Prof. Pranali Kashinath Kosamkar
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Dr. Pranali Kosamkar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Engineering and Technology at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. Prof. Pranali is well known for her fundamental and impactful contribution to the field of computing education, elevating every student she has mentored through a consistent, selfless teaching service for more than 20 years. Her research interests are in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Information Retrieval, and Quantum Computing. Prof. Pranali is also a well-respected technologist. She has delivered sessions on Big Data Analytics with Python to selected officers of the Indian Armed Forces at Military Institute of Training, Girinagar Pune. She has guided many undergraduate and graduate projects and has national and international paper publications to her credit. Her innovative teaching of basic courses like compiler design, database management system, data mining and principles of programming languages has built a high recognition among the student community in the institute MIT-WPU, Pune.

entries.title: Prof. Prashant Joshi
entries.excerpt: Dr. Prashant Joshi is an accomplished researcher and academician in the field of medicinal chemistry, specializing in natural product-based drug discovery. He earned his B. Pharm. from Mahakal Institute of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Ujjain (India) in 2006, followed by an M. Pharm. from the School of Pharmaceutical Sciences, RGPV Bhopal (India) in 2009. In 2018, he obtained his Ph.D. under the supervision of Dr. Sandip B. Bharate at CSIR-Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine, Jammu (India), through the Academy of Scientific and Innovative Research (AcSIR), New Delhi, as a CSIR-Senior Research Fellow. Dr. Joshi has held academic positions in pharmaceutical and natural product chemistry departments at SPTM, NMIMS Shirpur. Currently, he serves as an Associate Professor at the School of Health Science and Technology, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. His research interests focus on medicinal chemistry and natural products, aiming to discover new therapeutics for multi-drug-resistant (MDR) cancers and bacterial infections. He has an impressive record of 32 research publications and 9 US patents granted in the field of natural source-based drug discovery.

entries.title: Prof. Rajashri Amit Kadam
entries.excerpt: Dr. Rajashri Kadam is dedicated Associate Professor in the domain of Marketing and currently handling additional responsibility as the Associate Dean – Academics at the School of Business since January 2022. With over 22 years of combined industry and academic experience, she has been a passionate educator for the past 18 years, known for her interactive and learner- centric pedagogy. Dr. Kadam holds a Ph.D. in Marketing Management and an Executive Diploma in Digital Marketing from IIM Jammu. Her teaching interests include Advertising, Services Marketing, Integrated Marketing Communication, and Strategic Marketing. She has contributed significantly to academic research with publications in reputed national and international journals, Scopus-indexed book chapters, Case studies and numerous conference papers. She has also guided student-led case studies and research publications. She is also a Ph.D. supervisor and certified trainer, with prior experience in consulting and content development. Known for her strong rapport with students and alumni, Dr. Kadam continues to inspire learners with her dynamic teaching, academic leadership, and commitment to educational excellence.

entries.title: Prof. Rajib Kumar Sinharay
entries.excerpt: A geophysicist with total 25 years of experiences in industry as well as research mainly in geophysical investigations, hydrocarbon exploration, engineering geophysics, deep crustal studies and Antarctic research. Having a MScTech and PhD degree in Applied Geophysics from IIT(ISM), Dhanbad, I have worked for Central Water and Power Research Station (CWPRS), Govt. of India, WesternGeco- Schlumberger (SLB) and Reliance Industries Limited (RIL). Presently working as a Professor in School of Petroleum Engineering, Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University (MITWPU), Pune. My fields of researches are Petroleum Exploration and Production, Unconventional Hydrocarbon, Geothermal Exploration, Underground Carbon Sequestration (UCS), Machine Learning (ML), Mineral Exploration, Antarctic Geoscience and near surface geophysics. A passionate innovator, vigorous learner, motivated leader and critical thinker. Published more than 25 research paper in different peer-reviewed journals, books and conferences.

entries.title: Prof. Samarth Dilip Patwardhan
entries.excerpt: Samarth D. Patwardhan serves as Head of Centre for Subsea Engineering Research (CSER), and Professor in the Department of Petroleum Engineering at MITWPU, Pune, India. His research interests include production and reservoir engineering, enhanced oil recovery, HSE, as well as applying data analytics to the oil and gas domain. Through research grants he has procured; he has been instrumental in revamping the reservoir characterization laboratory and upgrading the simulation lab with industry-benchmarked software tools at the department. He is involved in collaborating with industry to further the academic and research partnerships for the recently commissioned CSER lab, a one-of-its-kind lab established through a common shared vision with Aker Solutions. Further, his current efforts are also directed towards setting up a stateof-the-art research lab for testing various characteristics of Nano-dusts, which span across industries. The lab is jointly funded by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India (GoI), and Techniche Engineering Private Limited (TEPL), an HSE company based out of Pune, India. He works closely with his undergraduate, graduate and doctorate students on topics spanning from characterizing various rock types using data analytics, to enhanced oil recovery, and applying computational fluid dynamics to problems being faced in the oil and gas industry. He serves as a reviewer on multiple prestigious peer-reviewed journals and funding agencies and is involved in collaborative work on research proposals on multiple platforms, including academia and industry, both at national and international levels. He is the recipient of the 2019 APJ Abdul Kalam MIT WPU Research and Innovation Award, and the SPE 2022 Regional Distinguished Achievement Award for Petroleum Engineering Faculty for South Asia and Pacific Region. Samarth serves as a Member on the Enhanced Recovery Committee (ERC), formed under the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG), Government of India (GoI), Director on the SPE International Mumbai Section board, Faculty Advisor to the MITWPU – SPE Student Chapter, and a Member on the Energy4Me committee formed for South Asia, under SPE International. Apart from serving as a SPE Student Chapter Faculty Advisor since 2014, he has volunteered for multiple activities within SPE, including acting in the capacity of a judge for Regional Student Paper Contest (RSPC) for all categories (U.G., P.G., and Ph.D.) since 2017, as well as volunteering under SPE Cares initiative. Through industry support, he has been instrumental in organizing the RSPC at the MITWPU campus in 2014, as well as planning and organizing multiple technical workshops, guest lectures and field visits for student members, keeping up with SPE’s mission of disseminating and exchanging knowledge concerning oil and gas industry, to build technical and professional competence in the next generation workforce, thereby bridging the gap between industry and academics.

entries.text: Prof. Samuel Jacob Kuruvilla Associate Professor Department of Liberal Arts Politics of the Middle EAST, International Relations, Political Theory, Indian Foreign Policy Dr. Samuel Jacob Kuruvilla is an Associate Professor at the MIT-WPU School of Liberal Arts. He completed his BA, majoring in Indian History (with minors in Economics & Political Science) from CMS College, Kottayam, followed by an MA in Politics & International Relations from the School of International Relations & Politics, Mahatma Gandhi University, Kottayam. He pursued his M.Phil in Diplomacy at Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU), New Delhi, and obtained his Ph.D. in Religion & Politics from the University of Exeter, United Kingdom. Previously, he served as a Post-Doctoral Fellow at the School of International Relations and Politics of Mahatma Gandhi University, Kottayam, Kerala. Additionally, he received his Doctorate in Politics and Theology from the University of Exeter in the United Kingdom.

entries.title: Prof. Shilpa Samir Chapadgaonkar
entries.excerpt: Bioprocess and enzyme technology, biopharmaceutical production and purification, tissue engineering, disease models, and sustainable biotechnology.
entries.text: Prof. Shilpa Samir Chapadgaonkar Associate Professor and Program Director Department of Biosciences and Technology Bioprocess and enzyme technology, biopharmaceutical production and purification, tissue engineering, disease models, and sustainable biotechnology. Dr. Shilpa S. Chapadgaonkar is an experienced academician and researcher with expertise in upstream processing, bioseparation, and enzyme technology. With over 15 years of teaching experience at UG, PG, and PhD levels, she has guided four PhD candidates to completion and is currently supervising three more, along with several M.Sc. and M.Tech. projects. She is leading the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MITWPU as Program Director. She has held key roles such as MSc (Biotech) Program Coordinator, Research Cluster Head, and PhD Program Coordinator, contributing significantly to NAAC and UGC audits. Her research focuses on drug discovery, bioprocess development for biopharmaceuticals, and investigating genetic disorders like infertility and autism. Passionate about sustainability, she has developed technologies for waste valorization, enzyme formulations, and textile wastewater treatment. Dr. Chapadgaonkar’s work has been supported by grants from the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), and she has led industrial collaborations with global companies like BASF and Gennova Biopharmaceuticals.

With areas of specialization in Materials, Composites, Nanocomposites, Manufacturing System, Renewable Energy Technologies, Power Generation, Waste Heat Recovery, Emission, Operations Research, Research Methodology, Applied Thermodynamics, Project Management, and Operations Research, he possesses exceptional skills and expertise. He has been honored with the MIT Ideal Teacher Award in September 2017 and elected as a Member of the Committee of the Institution of Engineers (India), Pune Local Center for the period of August 2021-July 2023. entries.title: Prof. Sushil Kumar Gupta
entries.excerpt: Dr. Sushil Kumar Gupta brings over 21 years of distinguished experience, equally divided between corporate leadership and academic excellence. A UGC-NET qualified professional in Management (June 2012), he holds a Ph.D. in Management from Amity University, Uttar Pradesh, and is an alumnus of IIM Ahmedabad, having completed the prestigious 43rd Faculty Development Programme in Pedagogy and Research Methods (April–May 2023). His academic portfolio includes 12 Scopus-indexed publications and 9 ABDC-listed papers (2 in category B and 7 in category C), alongside five patents in the domains of finance and behavioural analytics. He is also a recipient of a research grant from IIM Ahmedabad, further reinforcing his scholarly engagement. His research focuses on contemporary trends in finance, fintech, wealth management etc. He has been honoured with the Outstanding Educator Award 2025 by Lexicon Group of Institutions and the Global Teacher Award 2021 by AKS Education.

entries.title: Prof. Uma Ramachandra Pujeri
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Computer Networks, Cryptography, loud computing, encryption algorithm, Post Quantum Lattice algorithm
entries.text: Prof. Uma Ramachandra Pujeri Associate Professor Department of Computer Engineering and Technology Computer Networks, Cryptography, loud computing, encryption algorithm, Post Quantum Lattice algorithm Dr. Uma R Pujeri was born in Sangli, India in 1981. She earned her M.Tech degree from PSG Tech College of Engineering, Coimbatore in 2008 and received her doctorate degree from Anna University, Chennai in May 2017. Her research area focuses on computer network congestion control algorithms. With fourteen years of teaching experience, she has worked as an Assistant Professor at Adithya College of Engineering, Coimbatore for six years and is currently serving as an Associate Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, Maharashtra. Dr. Pujeri is a Life Member of the Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE) and has conducted several workshops, FDPs, and conferences. Her post-doctoral fellowship from Srinivas University, Mangalore, under the guidance of Dr. P.S. Aitthal, is registered under the ID 20SUPDROO9. Dr. Pujeri has published 40 research papers in international journals, and her PhD thesis is available on the UGC website https://shodhganga.inflibnet.ac.in/handle/10603/196148. Contact her at uma.pujeri@mitwpu.edu.in.

He completed his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from Savitribai Phule Pune University in 2018. He has published ten papers and has one granted and one published Indian patent. He is known for his expertise in the field of mechanical engineering and his passion for teaching and research.

Dr. Vanisree Ramanathan, Ph.D., Associate Professor at MIT World Peace University located in Pune. With an illustrious career spanning 19 years, she has made significant contributions in the fields of Sociology, Public Policy, Public Health, and Law. Dr. Vanisree has held full-time faculty positions at prestigious institutions including Symbiosis Law School, MIT School of Government in Pune, and Chinmaya Vishwavidyappeth in Cochin.Her expertise encompasses a wide array of interdisciplinary subjects including Public Health, Health Policy, Heal care laws Gender Studies, Education, sustainable Development, Good Governance, and Social Philosophy. Dr. Vanisree is renowned for her prolific research contributions, having authored and reviewed numerous scholarly papers in esteemed national and international journals. An accomplished writer and eloquent public speaker, Dr. Vanisree is known for her passionate advocacy and effective communication skills. She employs innovative teaching methodologies such as experiential learning and participatory developmen

entries.title: Prof. Varsha Shriram Nerlekar
entries.excerpt: Sustainability/ Public Finance/ Financial Technologies/ Behavioural Finance & Investments
entries.text: Prof. Varsha Shriram Nerlekar Associate Professor Department of Business Sustainability/ Public Finance/ Financial Technologies/ Behavioural Finance & Investments Dr. Varsha Nerlekar is an Associate Professor in Finance at the School of Management (PG) MIT World Peace University in Pune, India. With over 20 years of teaching and industry experience, her research interests include Sustainability, Public Finance, Financial Technologies, Behavioural Finance, and Investments. She received her Ph.D. in Management from Nagpur University, and her postgraduate degree from Indira Institute of Management, Pune. Dr. Nerlekar has published more than 20 research papers and case studies in national and international journals, and is a member of the Board of Studies for reputed institutes such as Symbiosis College of Commerce. She has also conducted Faculty Development Programs and Management Development Programs for various institutions.

entries.title: Prof. Vijay Kumar Kalyani
entries.excerpt: Neural networks, physics-informed neural networks, mathematical modelling, and mathematical seismology.
entries.text: Prof. Vijay Kumar Kalyani Professor of Practice Department of Mathematics and Statistics Neural networks, physics-informed neural networks, mathematical modelling, and mathematical seismology. Dr. Vijay Kumar Kalyani is currently serving as Professor of Practice at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. He is a distinguished mathematician and computational scientist with over 35 years of experience in teaching and research. He obtained his Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics from the Indian School of Mines (IIT), Dhanbad, in 1988. He also earned an M.Phil. with a Gold Medal and an M.Tech. by research in Computer Applications. His research focuses on Artificial Neural Networks, Physics-Informed Neural Networks, mathematical modelling, and seismic wave propagation. He has served in senior scientific positions at CSIR-CIMFR, Dhanbad, including Chief Scientist (Scientist-G). He has supervised Ph.D. and postgraduate theses and taught B.Tech. and M.Tech. students. He has published more than 25 research papers in SCI, Scopus, and international journals. His work includes numerical simulation, deep learning, and computational geophysics. He continues to contribute actively to advanced research and education in applied mathematics and neural networks.

entries.title: Prof. Vijaykumar Bilolikar
entries.excerpt: Prof Bilolikar has a rich academic and administrative experience of 28 years and is a professor of Operations Management at MIT-WPU. He has handled various administrative responsibilities such as Dean of Student Affairs, Controller of examination, NBA coordinator etc. He holds a Bachelors in Production Engineering from Government College of Engineering and a Masters in manufacturing engineering. He holds a doctoral degree from Indian Institute of Technology Bombay in the area of operations management. His papers have been published in reputed international conferences and journals including International Journal of Production Research, Benchmarking an International Journal, International Journal of Logistics and Management, Annals of operations research etc. He is on the reviewer board of various journals. His research interests include supply chain management, application of AI techniques to operations and project management, simulation of manufacturing systems, sustainable manufacturing and Industry 4.0 architecture.

entries.title: Prof. Vikram Shete
entries.excerpt: Dr. Vikram Shete is an accomplished entrepreneur, mentor, and educator with over 15 years of experience fostering innovation and building entrepreneurial ecosystems. As an Associate Professor of Entrepreneurship at MIT World Peace University and a mentor with premier institutions like Stanford Seed, Atal Incubation Centre, and BITS Pilani, he has empowered hundreds of aspiring founders. His flagship course, Entrepreneurship Blueprint, has enabled students to launch viable startups, some of which have secured incubation offers. Driven by a mission to nurture the next generation of entrepreneurs, Dr. Shete blends academic rigor with real-world startup insights to deliver transformative learning experiences.

entries.title: Rahul Gaikwad
entries.excerpt: Rahul Gaikwad is an illustrator and design tutor with over 7 years of combined experience in the education and creative industries. He holds a Bachelor's degree in Fine Art from Sir J.J. School of Art and a Master's in Illustration and Animation from Coventry University, UK. His professional work spans across diverse domains such as illustration, asset building, animation, character design, branding, graphic design and storyboarding. Showcasing his versatility and command over visual storytelling. Rahul's teaching approach blends industry insight with academic rigor, helping students build strong creative foundations. In 2012, he was honored as a young cartoonist by the legendary R.K. Laxman through a recognition by the Times of India, marking a significant early milestone in his career. Passionate about both craft and mentorship, Rahul continues to inspire and guide aspiring designers through his practice and pedagogy.

entries.title: Rashmi Sridhara
entries.excerpt: Rashmi Sridhara is a multidisciplinary Textile and Surface Designer, Print Maker, DIY Content Developer, and Educator with over a decade of professional experience in the design industry. A postgraduate from the National Institute of Design (NID), Paldi, and an undergraduate from the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT), Bangalore, she has worked extensively across sectors including fashion, craft research, home furnishings, and education. Her areas of academic and creative expertise include surface ornamentation, functional fashion, traditional textile crafts, and game-based design learning.

entries.title: Ravi Nikam
entries.excerpt: Mr. Ravi Nikam is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Media & Communication at MIT-WPU, bringing extensive experience from both the media industry and academia. He believes that meaningful learning is achieved through a balance of theoretical understanding and hands-on practice. His teaching philosophy emphasizes experiential learning, critical thinking, ethical storytelling, and industry relevance, empowering students to become creative, socially responsible media professionals. Passionate about documentary filmmaking and visual storytelling, he is committed to fostering innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and research-oriented learning, contributing to MIT-WPU's vision of developing globally competent, socially conscious, and future-ready students.

entries.title: Renuka Amit Mane
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Dr. Renuka Mane is an Assistant Professor at MIT World Peace University, Pune, with over 10 years of experience in Computer Engineering. She specializes in Brain-Computer Interface (BCI), IoT, Blockchain, and Network Security. She holds a PhD from Bharati Vidyapeeth (2023) and has published 20+ research papers in international journals and conferences. With multiple patents in BCI and network security, she actively mentors students and participates in faculty development programs. Her expertise includes machine learning, neural networks, and IoT frameworks, contributing significantly to research and innovation in emerging technologies.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Biosciences and Technology Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU. Research The Department of Biosciences and Technology integrates molecular and cellular science, biotechnology, microbiology, bioinformatics, and translational research. 01 Cancer, cell & regenerative biology Cancer cell biology, genome stability, stem cells, tissue engineering, bone biology, and protein aggregation. 02 Genomics, bioinformatics & microbiomes Genomics, transcriptomics, metagenomics, microbial and human microbiomes, protein structure, and computational biology. 03 Microbiology, immunology & bioprocessing Applied and environmental microbiology, immune profiling, microbial metabolites, enzyme technology, and bioprocess development. 04 Plant molecular biology & sustainable biotechnology Plant development, defence and metabolism; plant–microbe interactions; genome editing; and crop improvement. 05 Biosensors, diagnostics & bioimaging Electrochemical and optical biosensors, pathogen and molecular diagnostics, bioimaging, and preclinical workflows.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Chemistry Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU. Research The Department of Chemistry combines foundational chemical science and laboratory learning with faculty research in polymer science, synthesis and catalysis, functional and molecular materials, nanomaterials, environmental chemistry, and photonics. 01 Nanomaterials, molecular materials & photonics Nanomaterial synthesis, plasmonics and nanophotonics, metal-organic frameworks, molecular nanomagnets, switches, and fluorescent sensors. 02 Organic synthesis, catalysis & green chemistry Synthetic organic chemistry, ionic liquids, organocatalysis, heterogeneous catalysis, asymmetric synthesis, biocatalysis, and green synthesis. 03 Polymers, coatings & sustainable materials Liquid-crystalline and conducting polymers, biopolymers, nanocomposites, protective coatings, packaging, microplastics, and water purification. 04 Inorganic & environmental chemistry Metal complexes, wastewater treatment, pollutant degradation, and materials chemistry for environmental applications.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Environmental Studies Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU. Research The department offers postgraduate and doctoral study spanning ecology and biodiversity, water and pollution, climate and sustainability, environmental assessment, and applied field research. 01 Ecology, biodiversity & conservation Urban ecology, biogeography, species and habitat conservation, biodiversity informatics, and community-led restoration. 02 Water, pollution & environmental engineering River- and lake-bank filtration, emerging contaminants, water and wastewater treatment, and sustainable water systems. 03 Climate action & sustainability assessment Sustainable development goals, environmental impact assessment, life-cycle assessment, ESG, natural-resource management, and environmental innovation. 04 Citizen science & environmental communication Public participation, biodiversity data, environmental education, and science communication for conservation action.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Mathematics and Statistics Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT-WPU. Research The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering. 01 Discrete mathematics & combinatorics Matroid and graph theory, algebra, lattice theory, combinatorics, coding theory, and discrete structures. 02 Analysis, modelling & differential equations Geometric function theory, differential equations, numerical analysis, elastodynamics, wave propagation, fluid mechanics, and mathematical modelling. 03 Statistics, data science & machine learning Sampling, ranked-set sampling, statistical inference, Bayesian methods, statistical genetics, time series, biostatistics, data science, and machine learning. 04 Optimisation & operations research Optimisation, decision-making, transportation and supply-chain models, and fuzzy and rough sets.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Physics at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Physics Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Physics at MIT-WPU. Research The Department of Physics brings together teaching, laboratory learning, and research across fundamental, computational, optical, materials, and device physics. 01 Quantum, optics & photonics Nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, nanophotonics, photonic systems, plasmonics, and spectroscopy. Photonics Research Group Quantum Science & Technology Group 02 Materials, devices & nanoscience Semiconductor and dielectric thin films, nanomaterials and nanocomposites, sensors, photodetectors, and functional devices. 03 Astrophysics & fundamental physics Cosmic magnetic fields, galactic dynamics, high-energy theory, gravitation, and the mathematical structure of fundamental interactions. 04 Applied & computational physics Soft matter, quantum chemistry and computational physics; radiation processing of polymers; liquid acoustics; dielectric spectroscopy; and corrosion science.

entries.title: Rucha Deshpande
entries.excerpt: Generative AI, User Experience, Design Education
entries.text: Rucha Deshpande Assistant Professor Department of Design Generative AI, User Experience, Design Education Rucha Deshpande is a seasoned design educator with over 14 years of experience in design strategy, user experience, and creative thinking. She has worked with leading brands such as Lifestyles and Reliance Trends as a Concept Designer for retail store experiences before transitioning into academia with institutions including the Symbiosis Institute of Design and MIT-WPU, Pune. An alumna of NID Ahmedabad and a certified UX Designer from IIT Delhi, she brings a strong foundation in user-centred design and cross-functional collaboration.

entries.title: Sagar Sunil Kolapkar
entries.excerpt: Transportation materials, Warm Mix Asphalt, Parking Policy and Demand Studies
entries.text: Sagar Sunil Kolapkar Assistant Professor Department of Civil Engineering Transportation materials, Warm Mix Asphalt, Parking Policy and Demand Studies Prof. Sagar Kolapkar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering, MIT World Peace University, Pune. He received his B.E. in Civil Engineering (2010) from Pune University and M.Tech (Transportation Systems Engineering) in 2013 from IIT Guwahati. His research focuses on Parking Demand Management and Supply Regulation and Warm Mix Asphalt. He is a certified Road Safety Auditor by the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways and Indian Roads Congress. He has actively worked with Pune Municipal Corporation on Parking Policy. He qualified GATE in 2011 and has published several research papers in top-ranked journals in Civil Engineering. He has been an active reviewer of international journal Materials Today (Elsevier). He has written a book on Advanced Transportation Engineering. He is actively working as Infrastructure Cordinator at Department of Civil Engineering.

He earned his bachelor’s degree in civil engineering from the University of Pune in 2011, followed by a Master’s in Structural Engineering from MIT, Pune in 2013. He was awarded a Ph.D. in 2024, with a specialization in Corrosion in Reinforced Concrete.

entries.title: Seema Jitendra Patil
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Machine Learning
entries.text: Seema Jitendra Patil Assistant Professor Department of Computer Engineering and Technology Machine Learning Prof. Mrs. Seema J. Patil is a highly accomplished computer engineer, who graduated with distinction from SSBT's COET, Jalgaon in 2006. She completed her Master's degree from MIT Pune and went on to work as a lecturer in SMIT College, Jalgaon for a year. With a successful software testing training program from STC Technologies, Pune and an IBM certification in DB2 fundamental, she joined MIT-WPU as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Engineering, where she has been teaching for the past 15 years. Prof. Patil has a wide range of teaching experience and has guided many undergraduate level projects. She has also coordinated various subjects and organized industrial visits for students. Her research work has been published in several international journals. Prof. Patil's dedication to her students and passion for teaching has made her an inspiring mentor and guide.

Mr. Shailendra Dayane is currently working as Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering Department of Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Kothrud Pune. He is having more than 24 years of teaching experiance. He holds Masters of Engineering in Design Engineering Domain and Currently pursuing Ph. D. in Mechanical Engineering. Master's work was associated with peeling strength of different Biopolymers. Research work is going on Peeling and Fatigue Behavior of Adhesively Joint Different Biopolymers. A dedicated educator, he focuses on inspiring students to engage critically with literature while contributing meaningfully to the study of diverse perspectives. His commitment to fostering intellectual growth reflects his belief in the transformative power of education.

entries.text: Shiv Havgirao Sutar Assistant Professor Department of Computer Engineering and Technology Wireless Sensor Networks, Internet of Things, and High-Performance Computing. Prof. Shiv H. Sutar is an Assistant Professor at the School of Computer Engineering and Technology, MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. With over 15 years of experience in teaching, he is a flexible, dynamic, and enthusiastic professional in the field of wireless sensor networks, internet of things, and high-performance computing. He holds a technical background and has delivered lectures on topics such as compilers, operating systems, system software, and IoT. Prof. Sutar seeks challenging positions in technical institutes that require innovation, creativity, and dedication, enabling him to work in a fast-paced and challenging environment.

With over two years of teaching experience, she brings a creative and research-driven approach to the classroom. Her academic and creative interests lie at the intersection of visual arts and cultural studies. Her areas of interest include typography, visual storytelling, semiotics, and the role of visual media in preserving cultural narratives.

entries.title: Shubhangi Rajesh Shekokar
entries.excerpt: Prof. Shubhangi Shekokar is currently an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune. She received her B.E. in Civil Engineering from Government College of Engineering, Amravati in 2004 and her M. Tech in Geotechnical Engineering from College of Engineering Pune (COEP) in 2010. Presently, she has completed Ph.D. in Environmental Engineering from SGBAU, Amravati University under the guidance of Dr. Y.R.M. Rao. She is a life member of the Indian geotechnical Society (IGS), Institute of Public Health Engineers (IPHE) and the Indian Society of Technical Education (ISTE). She has been teaching at the institute since August 2008 and has experience teaching various subjects like BCE, Survey, Soil Mechanics, Foundation Engineering, Building Design and Drawing, Estimation and Costing, Valuation and Slope Stabilization Techniques. Dr. Shekokar has published 5 research papers in Scopus Indexed Journals, 2 in National Journals, and 6 in International Conferences.

Shubhika Malara is a passionate Visual Artist, Designer and Educator, with over 5 years of expertise in Communication and Outreach, and a strong interest in sustainability, public spaces, heritage and culture studies. As an Assistant Professor at MIT-WPU School of Design in Visual Communication Design, she facilitates courses on Design History, Strategy and Research Methods, Branding, Illustration, Typography and Publication Design. She is a strong advocate of impact-driven design solutions, and attempts to nurture lateral thinking and interdisciplinary approach to problem-solving.

entries.text: Smita Sanjay Paithankar Assistant Professor Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering electric Vehicle -Axial Flux Motor ,Eletrical Machines Prof. Smita S Paithankar is a highly qualified and experienced educator in the field of Electrical Engineering. She completed her B.E. in Electrical Engineering from Nagpur University in 2000, followed by an M.Tech in Integrated Power System from G.H.Raisoni College of Engineering in 2008. With over 9 years of teaching experience and 8 months of industry experience, Prof. Paithankar has a strong command over subjects such as Basic Electrical Engineering, Electrical Technology, Basic Electronics Engineering, and Power devices and Machines. She is an active member of the Institution of Engineers (IE) and the Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE). Prof. Paithankar has also been involved in various academic and administrative responsibilities throughout her career.

entries.title: Sudhanshu Jaiswal
entries.department: Department of Computer Science and Applications
entries.excerpt: Mr. Sudhanshu Jaiswal is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science & Applications under the School of Computer Science & Engineering. He holds a Master of Science in Blockchain Technology from MIT World Peace University, where he was awarded a Gold Medal, and a B.Tech in Information Technology from IIIT Vadodara. With one year of teaching experience and over four years as a Software Engineer, he brings valuable industry perspective to academia. His expertise includes Blockchain Technology, Cryptography, and Mathematics. He teaches Blockchain Technology & Applications, Ethereum Technology, Smart Contracts Programming, and Blockchain & IoT Applications, and has a keen interest in the entertainment industry, with two years of experience as an Assistant Director.

entries.title: Sulakshana Sagar Malwade
entries.excerpt: Prof. Sagar Kolapkar is an Assistant Professor in the School of Civil Engineering, MIT World Peace University, Pune. He received his B.E. in Civil Engineering (2010) from Pune University and M.Tech (Transportation Systems Engineering) in 2013 from IIT Guwahati. His research focuses on Parking Demand Management and Supply Regulation and Warm Mix Asphalt. He is a certified Road Safety Auditor by Ministry of Road Transport and Highways and Indian Roads Congress. He has actively worked with Pune Municipal Corporation on Parking Policy. He qualified GATE in 2011 and has published several research papers in top-ranked journals in Civil Engineering. He has been an active reviewer of international journal Materials Today (Elsevier). He has written a book on Advanced Transportation Engineering. He actively working as Infrastructure Cordinator at School of Civil Engineering.

entries.excerpt: behavioral economics, monetary economics, gender, and development.
entries.text: Swapnil Virendra Chalwadi Assistant Professor Department of Liberal Arts behavioral economics, monetary economics, gender, and development. Swapnil Chalwadi is an accomplished Assistant Professor and Program Head at the School of Liberal Arts in MIT WPU, Pune, India. With over 8 years of experience in teaching, he specializes in the macroeconomic and monetary aspects of economic development. He has earned an M.Phil degree in Economics from the University of Hyderabad and a Master's in Economics from Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune. Swapnil's deep interest and expertise in macroeconomics have helped him publish research papers in reputed journals, making him a valuable asset to the academic community.

Her detail-oriented approach, paired with exceptional communication skills, allows her to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and deliver top-notch design solutions, even under tight deadlines. Tejaswini thrives in fast-paced, dynamic environments, always eager to elevate projects to new heights. Whether working on a creative project or guiding students, her passion for design shines through in her ability to craft impactful visual experiences. Her commitment to innovation and design excellence makes her a valuable asset in any creative endeavor.

entries.title: Vandana Swapnil Jagtap
entries.department: Department of Computer Engineering and Technology
entries.excerpt: Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science
entries.text: Vandana Swapnil Jagtap Assistant Professor Department of Computer Engineering and Technology Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science Prof. Vandana Jagtap is an Assistant Professor at Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune, India. She has an M.Tech degree in Computer Science & Engineering from the University of Pune and is currently pursuing her Ph.D. in the same field. With 23 years of teaching experience, her areas of interest include Advanced Machine Learning, High-performance Computing, Distributed and Parallel computing, Cloud Computing, Algorithms, and Assistive Technology. Prof. Jagtap is the author of the book "Cloud Computing Policies, Outsourcing and Computing Keys: Cloud Computing policies" and has 50+ research publications in refereed international journals and conferences. She has also reviewed many refereed International journals and worked as an Advisory member and Editorial Board Member. Prof. Jagtap has delivered many expert lectures and is a member of various professional bodies such as ACM, CSI, ISTE, IEI. Under her guidance, 25 B.Tech (CSE) Project Groups and 13 M.Tech (CSE) Scholars have completed their projects.
